description: Batch prep this freezer door pepperon'tini, a martini with a twist, for your next party! Get a whole bunch super silky, spicy, herby 'tinis with very low effort.
ingredients: 1 ( 750 ml) bottle gin, such as Broker's London Dry
3 ounces dry Vermouth, such as Dolin's
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Pour 1 cup gin out of the 750 mLbottle. Set aside for another use. Add Vermouth, filtered water, and pepperoncini brine to the gin bottle.
Chill in the freezer for at least 4 hours, or until ready to serve. If desired, serve with a lemon twist garnish.
ingredient_text: bottle gin such as broker s london dry ounces dry vermouth such as dolin s ounces water ounces pepperoncini brine lemon twist

description: Try these big batch espresso martinis that are stored in your freezer. Sweet coffee liqueur, smooth vodka and fresh brewed espresso combine in this delicious martini variation.
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Pour out 1 ¼ cups of the vodka bottle. Reserve poured-out vodka for another use.

Pour 1/2 of the remaining vodka from the bottle into a small saucepan and warm over low heat for 1 to 2 minutes (do not boil). Remove from heat and immediately whisk in the espresso powder until fully dissolved and no lumps remain. Allow the mixture to cool for 5-10 minutes before proceeding.
Add the cooled vodka espresso mixture, coffee liquor, simple syrup, and a pinch of sea salt to the vodka bottle. Cap and shake gently to mix.
Freeze for at least 4 hours before serving. Note: mixture will not fully freeze due to the alcohol content.

To serve, fill a cocktail shaker with ice and add 3 ounces of the mixture. Dilute with ½ ounce water per individual serving. Shake vigorously for 15 to 20 seconds. Strain into a chilled martini or coupe glass. Garnish with 3 coffee beans or a dusting of espresso powder, if desired.
ingredient_text: bottle vodka coffee liqueur espresso powder simple syrup pinch sea salt water as needed ice coffee beans for garnish optional

description: The Bramble is a tart, refreshing gin cocktail that perfectly balances bright citrus with juicy berry flavors. Built on a foundation of London Dry Gin, it's shaken with fresh lemon juice and a touch of simple syrup, then crowned with a drizzle of crème de mûre (blackberry liqueur) for a beautiful layered effect. It's typically served over crushed ice and garnished with fresh blackberries and a slice of lemon.
directions: Add ice cubes, gin, lemon juice, and simple syrup to a cocktail shaker. Seal and shake vigorously until outside is frosted, 10 to 15 seconds.

Fill a rocks glass with crushed ice. Strain cocktail into glass, then gently pour crème de mûre over the top to create a layered effect. The crème de mûre will sink down to the bottom of the glass. Garnish with blackberries and a lemon slice.
ingredient_text: ice cubes ounces gin ounce lemon juice ounce simple syrup crushed ice ounce blackberry liqueur blackberries slice lemon
directions_text: add ice cubes, gin, lemon juice, and simple syrup to a cocktail shaker. seal and shake vigorously until outside is frosted, 10 to 15 seconds. fill a rocks glass with crushed ice. strain cocktail into glass, then gently pour crème de mûre over the top to create a layered effect. the crème de mûre will sink down to the bottom of the glass. garnish with blackberries and a lemon slice.
combined_text: bramble (cocktail) cocktails cocktails 1 cup ice cubes 2 ounces gin 1 ounce lemon juice 1/2 ounce simple syrup crushed ice 1/2 ounce blackberry liqueur) 2 or 3 blackberries 1 slice lemon add ice cubes, gin, lemon juice, and simple syrup to a cocktail shaker. seal and shake vigorously until outside is frosted, 10 to 15 seconds. fill a rocks glass with crushed ice. strain cocktail into glass, then gently pour crème de mûre over the top to create a layered effect. the crème de mûre will sink down to the bottom of the glass. garnish with blackberries and a lemon slice.

Fill a rocks glass with crushed ice. Strain cocktail into glass, then gently pour crème de mûre over the top to create a layered effect. The crème de mûre will sink down to the bottom of the glass. Garnish with blackberries and a lemon slice.

description: In this Italian cherry margarita, amaretto and fresh cherries join up with lime and tequila for a refreshing cocktail.
ingredients: 2 fresh cherries, pitted, or more to taste, plus more for garnish
directions: Place cherries and lime juice in a shaker and muddle to loosen the cherry juices.
Fill shaker with ice, add tequila and amaretto, and shake until well blended and slightly frothy. Strain over ice and garnish with a cherry.

description: Wake up the standard brandy Alexander. This version adds fresh-brewed coffee and complex, bittersweet amaro—and flexes as a warm or iced cocktail. You can use other types of amaro, such as Nonino Quintessentia or Montenegro.
directions: Fill a cocktail shaker half full with ice. Add coffee, brandy, crème de cacao, half-and-half, and Amaro Averna. Cover and shake until very cold.
Strain into a cocktail glass. Garnish with coffee beans and/ or nutmeg.

description: The flavors in this orange creamsicle margarita definitely take me back to my childhood of when the ice cream truck drove through my neighborhood on hot summer days. This tastes just like the push up orange ice creamsicle.
ingredients: 2 tablespoons orange zest, salt, and sugar mix (for sugar rim) (optional, see Note)
2 slices orange or lemon, for garnish
directions: Gather all your ingredients.
Mix orange zest, sugar, and salt together on a small plate
Rim 2 glasses with lime wedge and rotate in the orange zest mixture.
Combine tequila, orange juice, lime juice, triple sec, and ice cream in a cocktail shaker.
Fill cocktail shaker with ice and shake very well. Strain into the orange-rimmed glasses and garnish with an orange or lemon slice

description: Spicy but sweet. This pineapple jalapeño margarita has the best of both worlds!
ingredients: 2 slices fresh pineapple, plus more for garnish
directions: Combine pineapple and jalapeno slices in a cocktail shaker and muddle. Pour in tequila, pineapple juice, lime juice, and orange juice. Fill with ice and shake until well combined. Set aside.
Place tajin on a plate. Rub one side of glass with lime slice and roll in tajin. Strain margarita into the prepared glass and garnish with pineapple slice and jalapeno slice if desired.

description: This whipped vodka lemonade, with fresh lemon juice balanced by rich cream and a dollop of vanilla whipped cream is tart, sweet, and almost dessert. Perfect for sunny afternoons or after-dinner delight, this cocktail is sunshine in a glass.
ingredients: 2 fluid ounce lemon and elderflower vodka
directions: Combine limoncello cream, lemon juice, sweetened condensed milk, heavy cream, and lemon & elderflower vodka in a blender. Add ice; blend on Whip or Frozen Cocktail setting until ice is blended.
Pour into glasses; garnish with vanilla whipped cream, lemon zest, and dried or fresh lemon wheel.

description: This big batch of spiked Arnold Palmers is the best answer to a hot summer afternoon. Beat the heat with a ready batch of sweet tea, lemonade, and bourbon to serve over ice waiting in the refrigerator.
directions: Combine sweet tea, lemonade, and bourbon in a 1-gallon jug. Seal the jug and carefully tilt it upside down a couple time to mix the ingredients. Refrigerate until ready to serve; serve over ice.

description: This big batch Italian greyhound cocktail is made with gin, Aperol, and grapefruit juice, a refreshingly bitter and sweet combination that will please everyone at the party.
directions: Combine grapefruit juice, gin, Aperol, and simple syrup in a jug and seal tightly. Lightly shake jug to mix. Refrigerate until ready to serve.
Lightly shake and serve over ice; garnish with a rosemary sprig.

description: Here's a big batch freezer strawberry Cosmopolitan recipe to start your next party. Making strawberry simple syrup yourself is easy, and then all you need to do is combine it with vodka, lime juice and orange liqueur in the vodka bottle. Keep it in the freezer until ready to pour.
directions: Combine strawberries, sugar and 3/4 cup water in a small saucepan over medium-high heat. Bring to a boil, stirring occasionally. Reduce heat to a simmer and cook until mixture has thickened slightly and reduced a little, about 10 minutes.
Strain strawberry mixture into a jar and discard solids. Let cool completely and refrigerate until ready to use.

Pour 1 3/4 cups vodka from bottle and reserve for another use. To the remaining vodka, add 1/2 cup cooled strawberry simple syrup, orange liqueur, lime juice, and remaining 1/4 cup water. Seal the bottle and tilt it gently to mix the ingredients.
Place bottle in freezer for at least 6 hours or until ready to serve.
Lightly shake bottle before serving in a martini glass and garnish individual cosmos with a slice of lime if desired.

description: This mimosa recipe is made with fresh orange juice and Champagne, but you can use sparkling wine, if you prefer. Enjoy this delightful cocktail on Mother's Day, Christmas brunch, or any special occasion!

Divide orange juice evenly into two flute glasses.

Serve and enjoy!

description: This paloma martini combines tequila, fresh grapefruit juice, and grapefruit soda in a refreshing cocktail, perfect for summer nights on the patio.
ingredients: 1 grapefruit slice or lime slice or both
directions: Add tequila, lime juice, grapefruit juice, and agave nectar into a cocktail shaker. Add ice and shake until the outside of the shaker is frosted, about 30 seconds.
Pour into a frosted martini glass, leaving enough room for a splash of grapefruit soda. Garnish with a grapefruit slice or lime slice or both.

description: In this passion fruit margarita, bold tropical flavors take center stage in a twist on a classic margarita. Perfect for taco night, or relaxing on the patio on a warm day.
ingredients: 1 1/2 cups ice, divided, or as needed
directions: Sprinkle salt onto a plate. Moisten the rim of a glass with lime wedge and press the moistened rim into the salt; fill the glass with ice.

Fill a cocktail shaker half-full with ice. Add passion fruit juice, tequila, triple sec, and lime juice to the shaker. Seal and shake vigorously until outside is frosted, 10 to 15 seconds. Strain mixture into the glass and stir. Garnish with lime wedge.

description: This guava margarita will make you think you're on a tropical beach. Guava nectar adds a sweet, slightly floral note to the classic margarita flavor. Be careful, these go down easy!

Fill a cocktail shaker half-full with ice. Add tequila, triple sec, and lime juice to the shaker. Seal and shake vigorously until outside is frosted, 10 to 15 seconds. Strain mixture evenly between the glasses and stir. Garnish with remaining lime wedges.

description: Making a big batch of freezer margaritas couldn't be easier with this recipe. The margaritas are frozen right inside the tequila bottle.
directions: Pour 1 1/4 cups tequila from the bottle and reserve for another use.
Add lime juice, triple sec, agave, and salt to the bottle of tequila and place top on the bottle. Tilt bottle to lightly shake mixture until agave has been well incorporated.
Place bottle in the freezer for at least 8 hours or until ready to serve.

To serve, add 1 cup ice to a shaker or glass and top with about 1/2 cup of margarita mix. Shake mixture or pour margarita between two glasses until well blended and slightly frothy. Serve immediately with more ice if desired. This step is important to balance the taste through slight dilution from the ice.

description: The Batanga cocktail, with tequila, cola, and lime, in a salted glass is similar to a Cuba libre, but with tequila.
directions: Run a lime wedge around the edge of a glass and rim with salt if desired. Fill cup with ice and add tequila and lime juice Top with cola and add a pinch of salt. Stir and enjoy with a wedge of lime.

description: You can use any type of jam for this jammy vodka drink. Also a great way to use up the last bits of jam in the glass - just use the jam glass as your shaker.
ingredients: 1 tablespoon strawberry preserves, or more to taste
directions: Add strawberry preserves, tequila, orange liqueur, fresh lime juice, and ice to a jar fitted with a lid or shaker. Close jar and shake until slightly frothy.

description: In this green tea and honey-rhubarb punch, sweet honey and fresh mint balance rhubarb's tart flavor notes for a sparkling big-batch vodka sipper.
directions: For syrup, stir rhubarb, honey, water, and ginger together in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil; reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, for 15 minutes. Remove from heat; let cool completely, about 1 hour.
Strain through a fine-mesh sieve into an airtight container or jar; discard solids. Syrup can be stored in the refrigerator for up to 1 week.

For green tea punch, bring water to a boil in a medium saucepan. Remove from heat and add tea bags. Steep, covered, 5 minutes. Remove tea bags, or strain to remove loose tea. Let tea cool completely, about 1 hour.
Muddle mint in a large pitcher. Add cooled tea, vodka, honey-rhubarb syrup, and lime juice. Chill at least 30 minutes, or up to 12 hours.
Add club soda to a pitcher and stir gently. Serve over ice and garnish with fresh mint and rhubarb stalks.

description: Irish coffee is a delicious after-dinner cocktail. It's easy to make with freshly brewed coffee, Irish whiskey, and whipped cream. It's traditionally sweetened with brown sugar but Irish cream liqueur adds that little touch of luxury!

1 (1.5 fluid ounce) jigger Irish cream liqueur

Combine Irish whiskey and Irish cream liqueur in a glass mug; pour in hot brewed coffee.
Top with whipped cream and a dash of nutmeg.

description: This passion fruit fizz martini is a vibrant, effervescent cocktail that brings a taste of the tropics to your glass. You can find passionfruit pulp, with or without seeds, in the frozen aisle of most international markets.
directions: In a cocktail shaker, combine vodka, passion fruit pulp, simple syrup, lime juice and ice. Shake well until the outside of the shaker is frosted, about 15 seconds.

Strain into a martini glass. Use a Hawthorne cocktail strainer to retain passion fruit seeds in the drink, or use a fine mesh strainer to strain out seeds. Top with chilled Champagne, and garnish with lime wheel.

description: This tropical passion fruit mojito uses frozen passion fruit pulp for a tangy, fruity kick. Fresh mint, lime juice, simple syrup, rum, and club soda make it refreshing and easy to prepare. You can find passionfruit pulp (with or without seeds) in the frozen aisle of most international markets.
directions: In a cocktail shaker, combine lime juice, simple syrup and 6 mint leaves. Use a muddler to crush the leaves for about 10 seconds. Add rum and 1 cup ice. Seal shaker and shake vigorously until outside is frosted, 10 to 15 seconds.
Strain mojito into a glass. Add passion fruit pulp and club soda and gently stir. Fill glass with crushed ice and garnish with remaining mint leaves.

description: This ranch Bloody Mary is so simple—just rim the glass with dry ranch dressing mix and add a little more ranch to the usual roster of ingredients for a refreshing and tasty twist.
ingredients: 2 dashes hot sauce, such as Tabasco®
directions: Place 1/2 teaspoon dry ranch dressing mix on a plate. Run a piece of lime around the rim of glass. Dip rim in dressing mix until coated. Set aside.

description: This citrus adobo Margarita combines the bright flavors of fresh lime and orange juice with the smoky, spicy essence of adobo sauce. It is the perfect blend of zesty, spicy, and refreshing! For an extra smoky kick, use half silver tequila and half mezcal.
directions: Sprinkle chili lime seasoning onto a plate. Moisten the rim of a glass with a lime wedge. Press the moistened rim into the seasoning. Fill the glass with ice.
Fill a cocktail shaker with tequila, triple sec, lime juice, orange juice, adobo sauce, and agave syrup. Add 1 cup ice. Seal and shake vigorously until outside is frosted, 10 to 15 seconds.
Strain margarita into the glass and garnish with remaining lime wedge.

description: The Bloody Caesar, an iconic Canadian cocktail simply referred to as a Caesar in Canada, is a national favorite, known for its distinctive flavor that sets it apart from the classic Bloody Mary. It combines vodka with Clamato juice (a savory mix of clam and tomato juice) for a distinct briny, unami taste. Spiced with hot sauce and Worcestershire sauce, it's usually garnished with a celery stick, lime wedge, pickled treats such as beans or olives, or whatever your heart desires.
directions: Sprinkle celery salt onto a plate. Moisten the rim of a glass with lime wedge. Press the moistened rim into the salt. Fill glass with ice.

Add vodka, Worcestershire sauce, and Tabasco sauce, then fill with Clamato juice and gently stir. Sprinkle in a pinch of celery salt, then garnish with lime wedge, celery stick and other preferred garnishes.

description: You'll love this green apple martini if green apples are your thing. It's a very simple, sour, and delicious version of this traditional cocktail.
ingredients: 2 fluid ounces green apple pucker liqueur
1 tablespoon agave syrup or simple syrup
directions: Add green apple vodka, green apple pucker, lemonade, and agave syrup to a cocktail shaker. Fill the shaker with ice.
Shake very well and pour into 2 frosted martini glasses. Garnish with green apple slices.

description: For this blackberry margarita, fresh blackberries are muddled to release their vibrant juices, then combined with smooth tequila, orange liqueur, simple syrup and a splash of lime juice, making this margarita both bold and refreshing.
directions: Sprinkle salt onto a plate. Moisten the rim of a glass with lime wedge. Press the moistened rim into the salt. Fill the glass with ice.

Fill a cocktail shaker with 10 blackberries, reserving 2 blackberries for garnish. Use a muddler to crush berries to release their juices. Add tequila, triple sec, simple syrup and lime juice to the shaker and add 1 cup of ice. Seal and shake vigorously until outside is frosted, 10 to 15 seconds.
Strain margarita into the glass and garnish with remaining blackberries.

Strain margarita into the glass and garnish with remaining blackberries.

description: Dolly Parton and Rachel Parton George share their unique twist on a classic martini in their cookbook "Good Lookin' Cookin'." In addition to olives, the sisters add in iced tea and pickled vegetables.
ingredients: 1/4 ounce brine from Mezzetta Italian Mix Giardiniera
vegetable pieces from Mezzetta Italian Mix Giardiniera, for garnish
directions: Combine vodka, tea, and brine in an ice-filled mixing glass; stir well to chill.
Fill a lowball or rocks glass with ice. Strain martini over ice. Garnish with olives and vegetable pieces.

description: This blackberry mojito adds fresh blackberries to rum, lime, and mint for a refreshing twist.
directions: Place sugar, lime juice, blackberries and mint leaves in an old fashioned glass, and mash well with a cocktail muddler. Add soda water and rum; stir. Fill with ice.

description: This spicy skinny Margarita is made with fresh orange juice and lime juice and sweetened with agave. I used habanero Tajin and floated jalapeno slices to make it even spicier.
directions: Pour Tajin onto a plate. Rub a lime wedge around the rim of a glass, and dip the rim of the glass in Tajin.

Pour in tequila, lime juice, orange juice, and agave syrup; cover and shake until the outside of the shaker has frosted, about 30 seconds. Strain into a chilled glass and garnish with remaining jalapeno slices. Serve immediately.

description: A classic sidecar calls for Cognac, orange liqueur, and lemon juice, but this PB&J-inspired rendition subs in peanut butter whiskey and adds raspberry liqueur. Top it off with sparkling water for an effervescent cocktail with a hint of nostalgia.
directions: Spread sugar on a small plate. Rub rims of 2 cocktail glasses with lemon wedge. Dip rims in sugar to coat.

Fill a cocktail shaker half full with ice. Add whiskey, lemon juice, orange liqueur, and raspberry liqueur. Cover and shake until outside of shaker is frosted, about 60 seconds. Strain into prepared glasses. Top each cocktail with 1 1/2 ounces sparkling water and garnish with a raspberry.

recipe_title: Big Batch Freezer Lemon Drop Martinis
description: These big batch freezer lemon drop martinis, easily made right in a half-empty vodka bottle, are ready when you are—stash the bottle in the freezer and serve them up any time.
directions: Pour vodka, lemon juice, orange liqueur, and simple syrup into a freezer-safe 750ml bottle. Cover tightly, and tilt gently a few times to incorporate ingredients. Place bottle in the freezer for several hours until very well chilled.
Shake gently before serving. Serve as desired, as in a sugar-rimmed glass. Store any extra in the freezer until ready to use.
ingredient_text: vodka freshly squeezed lemon juice orange liqueur simple syrup

description: This frozen espresso martini is perfect for hot weather and as a pick-me-up after a hectic work day. After all, it's 100 degrees and 5 o'clock somewhere!
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Stir brewed espresso and simple syrup together in a small pitcher. Pour mixture into ice cube trays and freeze for 8 hours or overnight.
Pour vodka, Kahlúa, and Frangelico into a blender. Add in the espresso ice cubes, and blend until smooth.
Pour frozen mixture into 4 martini glasses. Lightly dust the martini with black onyx cocoa powder. Enjoy!

Pour frozen mixture into 4 martini glasses. Lightly dust the martini with black onyx cocoa powder. Enjoy!

recipe_title: This Matcha Martini Will Be Your Go-to Drink of the Summer
description: Cool off with this creamy, vodka cocktail.
directions: Gather all ingredients.
In a cocktail shaker filled about 3/4 with ice, add prepared matcha, vodka, creme de cacao, and simple syrup.
Top with the lid, and shake for 1 to 2 minutes, or until frothy.
Strain into a cocktail glass and garnish with more matcha powder, if desired.

description: This creamy spiked Brazilian lemonade made with coconut rum and sweetened condensed milk is a delicious refreshing summer drink.
directions: Rinse limes and cut the ends off. Cut each lime in 8 wedges and add to a blender. Add water, coconut rum, and sweetened condensed milk; blend until creamy.
Fill 3 glasses with ice and strain lemonade over.

description: This celery margarita is a fun and unexpected riff on a classic margarita using a homemade celery cordial. A cordial is a sweetened spirit or a nonalcoholic combination of sugar, water, spices, and fruits or veggies.
directions: Mix salt and coriander together on a small plate. Wipe rim of a margarita glass with lime wedge. Dip rim in salt mixture to coat. Fill glass with crushed ice.

Fill a cocktail shaker half full with ice. Add tequila, celery cordial, lime juice, and bitters to shaker; cover and shake until outside of shaker is frosted, about 60 seconds. Strain into prepared glass and garnish with celery stalk.

description: This mint julep sweet tea is a refreshing, cool-me-down drink made with bourbon and sweet tea. I muddle 4 mint leaves in each glass for extra mint flavor. It's optional, but I think it really adds to the drink.
directions: Bring water, the 3/4 cup mint, and sugar to a boil in a saucepan. Remove from heat and add tea bags. Steep for 1 hour.
Strain mixture into a pitcher and add bourbon. Stir until combined.
Muddle 4 mint leaves in a glass. Fill halfway with ice, add a lemon slice, and fill with more ice. Add tea bourbon mixture; garnish with a mint sprig and a lemon wedge.

description: This watermelon sour patch is a surprise—it uses strawberry schnapps, but with the added vanilla vodka and sweet and sour mix it takes just like watermelon sour patch candy.
ingredients: 2 fluid ounces sweet and sour mix
directions: Pour 1/4 to 1/2 inch of sugar onto a small, shallow plate. Moisten rim of a rocks glass with a wedge of lime, dip the moistened glass into sugar; set aside.
Scoop ice into a shaker. Pour in strawberry schnapps, vanilla vodka, and sweet and sour mix; cover and shake until the outside is frosted. Strain into a chilled glass.

description: This Thai basil mojito is a simple yet exciting twist on the classic mojito, swapping out mint for fresh Thai basil.
directions: Fill a collins glass with ice and set aside.

In a cocktail shaker, combine lime juice, simple syrup and Thai basil leaves. Use a muddler to crush the leaves for about 10 seconds. Add rum and 1 cup of ice. Seal and shake vigorously until outside is frosted, 10 to 15 seconds.
Strain mojito into the glass and top with club soda; gently stir. Garnish with lime wedge and Thai basil sprig, if desired.

description: For this strawberry jalapeno margarita, you'll muddle fresh strawberries and jalapeno to spice up your margarita game! Garnish with a strawberry slice and enjoy the sweet heat of this delicious cocktail.
directions: Sprinkle salt onto a plate. Moisten the rim of a glass with lime wedge. Press the moistened rim into the salt. Fill the glass with ice.

Fill a cocktail shaker with jalapeno slices and strawberries, reserving 1 slice of strawberry for garnish. Use a muddler to crush the jalapeno and strawberries to release their juices. Add tequila, triple sec, lime juice and simple syrup to the shaker and add 1 cup of ice. Seal and shake vigorously until outside is frosted, 10 to 15 seconds.
Strain margarita into the glass and garnish with remaining strawberry slice, if desired.

description: This spicy mango margarita is a vibrant cocktail that blends sweet mango nectar with a kick of crushed jalapeño - the perfect balance of sweet, spicy, and tangy. Feel free to add extra jalapeno if you're craving more heat.
ingredients: 3 jalapeno slices, or more to taste
3/4 fluid ounces triple sec or other orange liqueur
directions: Sprinkle chili lime seasoning onto a plate. Moisten the rim of a glass with lime wedge. Press the moistened rim into the seasoning. Fill the glass with ice.

Fill a cocktail shaker with tequila and 2 jalapeno slices. Use a muddler to crush the peppers. Add mango nectar, triple sec, and lime juice to the shaker and add 1 cup of ice. Seal and shake vigorously until outside is frosted, 10 to 15 seconds.
Strain margarita into the glass and garnish with remaining jalapeno slice, if desired.

description: Crème de violette and lavender bitters bring floral flavors to this simple spring cocktail.
directions: Fill a cocktail shaker half full with ice. Add gin, egg white, lemon juice, simple syrup, crème de violette, and bitters. Cover and shake until outside of shaker is frosted, about 30 seconds.
Strain into a measuring cup; discard ice. Return cocktail to shaker; cover and shake until pressure builds and mixture starts to emulsify, about 45 seconds.
Pour club soda into a glass. Slowly strain cocktail into glass, allowing foam to rise. Garnish with lemon wheel.

description: Treat yourself to a coffee nudge cocktail topped with cream and chocolate sprinkles. It'll warm you from the inside out on a cold, blustery day!
directions: You will need 8 mugs or glass coffee cups. Pour 1 ounce of coffee liqueur into each mug, then add one ounce of brandy and 1/2 ounce of creme de cacao to each serving. Fill each mug with hot coffee, garnish with a dollop of whipped cream, and top with chocolate sprinkles; serve.

description: Have you ever had a tamarind margarita? This twist on the classic margarita has the perfect sweet-tart flavor from the tamarind paste. Add an extra 1/2 oz agave nectar if you like things a little sweeter.

description: Batch prep this freezer door pepperon'tini, a martini with a twist, for your next party! Get a whole bunch super silky, spicy, herby 'tinis with very low effort.
ingredients: 1 ( 750 ml) bottle gin, such as Broker's London Dry
3 ounces dry Vermouth, such as Dolin's
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Pour 1 cup gin out of the 750 mLbottle. Set aside for another use. Add Vermouth, filtered water, and pepperoncini brine to the gin bottle.
Chill in the freezer for at least 4 hours, or until ready to serve. If desired, serve with a lemon twist garnish.

description: Try these big batch espresso martinis that are stored in your freezer. Sweet coffee liqueur, smooth vodka and fresh brewed espresso combine in this delicious martini variation.
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Pour out 1 ¼ cups of the vodka bottle. Reserve poured-out vodka for another use.

Pour 1/2 of the remaining vodka from the bottle into a small saucepan and warm over low heat for 1 to 2 minutes (do not boil). Remove from heat and immediately whisk in the espresso powder until fully dissolved and no lumps remain. Allow the mixture to cool for 5-10 minutes before proceeding.
Add the cooled vodka espresso mixture, coffee liquor, simple syrup, and a pinch of sea salt to the vodka bottle. Cap and shake gently to mix.
Freeze for at least 4 hours before serving. Note: mixture will not fully freeze due to the alcohol content.

To serve, fill a cocktail shaker with ice and add 3 ounces of the mixture. Dilute with ½ ounce water per individual serving. Shake vigorously for 15 to 20 seconds. Strain into a chilled martini or coupe glass. Garnish with 3 coffee beans or a dusting of espresso powder, if desired.
ingredient_text: bottle vodka coffee liqueur espresso powder simple syrup pinch sea salt water as needed ice coffee beans for garnish optional

description: This pickle martini is surprisingly good! The tangy juice of the dill pickle suits the dry flavor of vodka.
ingredients: 1 ½ fluid ounces good-quality dill pickle juice
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Pour ice into a cocktail shaker; pour in vodka and dill pickle juice. Cover the shaker and shake vigorously for about 20 seconds; strain into martini glasses.
Garnish each cocktail with a dill pickle spear.

description: This paloma martini combines tequila, fresh grapefruit juice, and grapefruit soda in a refreshing cocktail, perfect for summer nights on the patio.
ingredients: 1 grapefruit slice or lime slice or both
directions: Add tequila, lime juice, grapefruit juice, and agave nectar into a cocktail shaker. Add ice and shake until the outside of the shaker is frosted, about 30 seconds.
Pour into a frosted martini glass, leaving enough room for a splash of grapefruit soda. Garnish with a grapefruit slice or lime slice or both.

description: This lemon drop martini cocktail is easy to make at home with vodka and freshly squeezed lemon juice for a refreshing sweet and sour cocktail. Sweeten with sugar to taste. It just doesn't get better than this!
ingredients: 1 teaspoon white sugar or more to taste
directions: Gather the ingredients.
Combine lemon juice, vodka, and sugar in a mixing glass and stir until dissolved; pour into martini glasses filled with crushed ice.
Garnish with a lemon slice and sprig of mint.

description: This passion fruit fizz martini is a vibrant, effervescent cocktail that brings a taste of the tropics to your glass. You can find passionfruit pulp, with or without seeds, in the frozen aisle of most international markets.
directions: In a cocktail shaker, combine vodka, passion fruit pulp, simple syrup, lime juice and ice. Shake well until the outside of the shaker is frosted, about 15 seconds.

Strain into a martini glass. Use a Hawthorne cocktail strainer to retain passion fruit seeds in the drink, or use a fine mesh strainer to strain out seeds. Top with chilled Champagne, and garnish with lime wheel.

description: This eggnog martini, with eggnog, vodka, and Irish cream, is creamy and delicious, a perfect cocktail for holiday parties.
ingredients: 1 1/2 luid ounce Irish cream liqueur, such as Bailey's®
directions: Pour brown sugar onto a plate. Pour a splash of eggnog on another plate and dip the rim of the glass in eggnog and then in brown sugar.

Fill a cocktail shaker with ice. Add eggnog, vodka, and Baileys. Close the shaker and shake until the outside of shaker is frosted, about 30 seconds. Pour into the prepared glass and sprinkle with cinnamon.

description: Shelia's chocolate martini definitely satisfies a sweet tooth, especially if you're not big on dessert. It's simple, with just a few ingredients, but delicious.
directions: Pour chocolate syrup into 2 martini glass; swirl to line the glass, and pour the extra into a cocktail shaker. Place glasses in the freezer until frosted, 5 to 10 minutes.
Add vodka, RumChata, and milk, to the cocktail shaker. Add ice and shake until the outside of the shaker is frosted, 10 to 15 seconds. Strain into coated martini glasses and enjoy.

description: You'll love this green apple martini if green apples are your thing. It's a very simple, sour, and delicious version of this traditional cocktail.

recipe_title: Big Batch Freezer Lemon Drop Martinis
description: These big batch freezer lemon drop martinis, easily made right in a half-empty vodka bottle, are ready when you are—stash the bottle in the freezer and serve them up any time.
directions: Pour vodka, lemon juice, orange liqueur, and simple syrup into a freezer-safe 750ml bottle. Cover tightly, and tilt gently a few times to incorporate ingredients. Place bottle in the freezer for several hours until very well chilled.
Shake gently before serving. Serve as desired, as in a sugar-rimmed glass. Store any extra in the freezer until ready to use.
ingredient_text: vodka freshly squeezed lemon juice orange liqueur simple syrup
directions_text: pour vodka, lemon juice, orange liqueur, and simple syrup into a freezer-safe 750ml bottle. cover tightly, and tilt gently a few times to incorporate ingredients. place bottle in the freezer for several hours until very well chilled. shake gently before serving. serve as desired, as in a sugar-rimmed glass. store any extra in the freezer until ready to use.

description: This frozen espresso martini is perfect for hot weather and as a pick-me-up after a hectic work day. After all, it's 100 degrees and 5 o'clock somewhere!
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Stir brewed espresso and simple syrup together in a small pitcher. Pour mixture into ice cube trays and freeze for 8 hours or overnight.
Pour vodka, Kahlúa, and Frangelico into a blender. Add in the espresso ice cubes, and blend until smooth.
Pour frozen mixture into 4 martini glasses. Lightly dust the martini with black onyx cocoa powder. Enjoy!

Pour frozen mixture into 4 martini glasses. Lightly dust the martini with black onyx cocoa powder. Enjoy!

recipe_title: This Matcha Martini Will Be Your Go-to Drink of the Summer
description: Cool off with this creamy, vodka cocktail.
directions: Gather all ingredients.
In a cocktail shaker filled about 3/4 with ice, add prepared matcha, vodka, creme de cacao, and simple syrup.
Top with the lid, and shake for 1 to 2 minutes, or until frothy.
Strain into a cocktail glass and garnish with more matcha powder, if desired.

directions: Rim martini glass with icing and sprinkles; set aside.
Fill a cocktail shaker with ice; pour in rum cream, vodka, and amaretto. Cover and shake until the outside of shaker has frosted. Strain into a chilled glass.

directions: Fill a cocktail shaker with ice; add cranberry juice, vodka, orange liqueur, and vermouth. Cover and shake until the outside of the shaker has frosted. Strain into 2 chilled glasses and garnish with cranberries.

description: If you like dirty martinis but don't want to take the time to prepare one fresh, just make a big batch ahead of time and keep in the freezer!
directions: Pour 6 ounces of vodka from the bottle and set aside for another use.
To the reserved vodka in the bottle, add vermouth, brine, and water. Put the top on the bottle and invert bottle to incorporate the ingredients.
Place bottle in the freezer for at least 4 hours and up to 2 months.
To serve, pour desired amount into chilled glass and garnish with olives.

Combine vodka and dry vermouth in a cocktail mixing glass. Fill with ice and stir until chilled.
Strain into a chilled martini glass.
Garnish with three olives on a toothpick.

description: A dirty martini is the perfect cocktail for olive lovers. It can be made with vodka or gin and served on the rocks or chilled.
ingredients: 1 fluid ounce brine from olive jar
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Stir together vodka, dry vermouth, brine, and olives in a mixing glass. Pour into a glass or shaker with ice.
Serve on the rocks, or strain into chilled cocktail glasses.
Enjoy!

description: This pumpkin martini is a fun, delicious cocktail for your next holiday gathering. A good friend shared this great idea!
ingredients: 1 (1.5 fluid ounce) jigger vanilla flavored vodka (such as Stoli)
1 (1.5 fluid ounce) jigger Irish cream liqueur (such as Bailey's)
1 (1.5 fluid ounce) jigger pumpkin flavored liqueur (such as Hiram Walker)
directions: Gather the ingredients.
Fill a cocktail shaker with ice.
Pour vanilla-flavored vodka, Irish cream liqueur, and pumpkin-flavored liqueur over ice. Cover and shake until the outside of the shaker has frosted.
Strain into a chilled martini glass. Garnish with ground cinnamon and ground nutmeg.

description: This refreshing watermelon martini is easy to make for the best summer drink ever!
directions: Combine salt and sugar in a small bowl and pour onto a small, shallow plate. Moisten the rims of 2 glasses with a piece of watermelon and dip the moistened glass rims into the salt and sugar mixture; set aside.

Pour watermelon juice, vodka, watermelon schnapps, simple syrup, and lime juice into a cocktail shaker over ice. Cover and shake until the outside of the shaker has frosted. Strain into the prepared glasses.

description: This 'martini' is perfect for fans of Earl Grey tea!
ingredients: 1 (1.5 fluid ounce) jigger fresh lemon juice
directions: Sprinkle the tea leaves over the gin in a small glass, and set aside to steep for 2 hours.
Pour 1/4 to 1/2 inch of white sugar onto a small, shallow plate. Moisten the rims of 2 martini glasses with a wedge of lemon, dip the moistened glasses into the sugar; set aside.
Strain the infused gin, lemon juice, and simple syrup into a cocktail shaker over ice. Cover, and shake until the outside of the shaker has frosted. Strain into the rimmed glasses to serve.

ingredients: ¾ (1.5 fluid ounce) jigger vanilla flavored vodka

directions: Place graham cracker crumbs in a shallow dish. Coat rim of martini glass with honey, and dip into graham cracker crumbs to coat.

description: Meyer lemons, a cross between a lemon and an orange, have a sweeter, less acidic flavor than the more common lemon. If you have to use a regular lemon, add a little extra sugar to taste. For the ultimate martini, use good quality vodka and orange liqueur. I should have named this the 'come to mama' martini!
ingredients: 1 fluid ounce orange-flavored liqueur (such as Cointreau®)
1 teaspoon white sugar, or as needed for rimming
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Combine 2 teaspoons sugar and warm water in a cocktail shaker; stir to dissolve sugar.
Pour Meyer lemon juice, lemon peels, vodka, and orange liqueur into the shaker; add ice. Cover and shake vigorously.
Remove a lemon peel from the shaker and wipe the rim of a martini glass with the peel. Dip the rim of the glass in 1 teaspoon sugar, or as needed.
Strain martini into prepared glass and enjoy!
ingredient_text: white sugar warm water meyer lemon juiced peeled fluid ounces vodka fluid ounce orange-flavored liqueur such as cointreau ice white sugar as needed for rimming

description: For the best martini, use a high-quality gin in this adult beverage. A twist of lemon zest is an alternative to the olive garnish.
directions: Fill a cocktail shaker with fresh ice cubes. Add about 1/2 ounce each dry and sweet vermouth. Shake enough to coat ice and pour off liquid. Add 8 ounces gin to coated ice in the shaker and shake vigorously. Pour into 4 chilled martini glasses, garnish with pimiento-stuffed olive and serve.

description: This Milky Way martini is a wickedly good and dangerous drink that'll leave you seeing stars!
ingredients: 2 fluid ounces white creme de cacao
directions: Chill a martini glass by filling it with 1/2 cup of ice and cold water.
Place 1 cup of ice cubes into a cocktail shaker. Pour vanilla-flavored vodka, white creme de cacao, and Irish cream liqueur over ice; cover and shake vigorously.
Dump ice and water from the martini glass and drizzle the inside of the glass with chocolate syrup. Strain cocktail into the glass to serve.

description: These blueberry martinis are the hit of any party! Easy to make, but the key is the homemade blueberry vodka that requires two weeks to infuse properly. After that, it's smooth and fruity sailing with a splash of lime juice!
directions: To make blueberry vodka: Pour about 1/3 of the bottle of vodka into a holding container; set aside. Score each blueberry with a small nick and place into the vodka bottle. Fill the vodka bottle with reserved vodka until just below the neck. Add just enough raspberry liqueur to top off the bottle. Let sit in a dark place for 2 weeks.

To make martini: Fill a cocktail shaker with ice. Add 2 parts blueberry vodka, 1 part raspberry liqueur, and a dash of lime juice to the shaker. Shake vigorously and strain into a glass. Garnish with twist of lime zest.
ingredient_text: liter vodka pint blueberries rinsed dried raspberry flavored liqueur lime juiced twist lime zest garnish

To make martini: Fill a cocktail shaker with ice. Add 2 parts blueberry vodka, 1 part raspberry liqueur, and a dash of lime juice to the shaker. Shake vigorously and strain into a glass. Garnish with twist of lime zest.

description: This raspberry lemon drop martini is similar to a standard lemon drop (which is, without a doubt my go-to drink on hot summer days!), but it's made with muddled fresh raspberries. I was inspired to create this recipe when my kids threw fresh home-grown raspberries into their lemonade. Tart, sweet, and refreshing, this cocktail is a winner.
ingredients: 10 fresh raspberries, or more to taste, divided
directions: Place 8 raspberries, lemon juice, and sugar into a cocktail shaker; mash well with a cocktail muddler. Add vodka and ice. Cover, and shake until the outside of the shaker has frosted. Strain into a chilled martini glass; garnish with a lemon wedge and remaining 2 raspberries.

description: This tropical martini recipe yields a dessert in a glass! The grenadine looks beautiful and balances out the fruit juices to give it a wonderful full-body flavor, so don't skip this step! Garnishes such as a whole strawberry on the side of the glass, a chunk of fresh pineapple, etc., are welcome.

description: After living in Austin Texas for 10 years, my husband and I perfected the perfect margarita. The secret to success is fresh fruit juice only and it doesn't use any pre-made mixes! Don't skimp and try buying juice. Squeeze it yourself or you won't get the best taste! If you are a real olive lover, add pimento stuffed olives and call it a 'Mexican martini'...delicious! and you won't confuse yours with anyone else's if you add 3 olives on a stirrer. :)
ingredients: 2 (1.5 fluid ounce) jiggers freshly squeezed lime juice
1 (1.5 fluid ounce) jigger freshly squeezed orange juice
1 (1.5 fluid ounce) jigger freshly squeezed lemon juice
directions: Place the ice cubes into a margarita glass. Pour in the tequila, Cointreau, and Grand Marnier. Pour in the lime juice, orange juice, and lemon juice to taste. Garnish with a pimento stuffed olive if desired.

description: Martini made with Thai basil. Add a sugar rim if you like.
directions: Place 2 lemon wedges, 4 Thai basil leaves, and sugar in a cocktail shaker and mash well with a cocktail muddler. Pour in vodka and ice cubes; cover and shake until the outside of the shaker has frosted, about 45 seconds. Strain into 2 chilled martini glasses and garnish each with 1 lemon wedge and 1 Thai basil leaf.

description: This Italian margarita recipe blends amaretto liqueur, tequila, fresh orange juice, and limeade with ice to make a fun and frozen twist on the classic cocktail. Frosty glasses rimmed with amaretto and sugar make this delicious drink even better!
ingredients: 4 fluid ounces amaretto liqueur, plus more for garnish
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Dip the rims of 4 margarita glasses in amaretto, then into sugar; set aside.
Pour limeade, tequila, amaretto, orange juice, and ice into a blender; blend until smooth.
Pour into prepared margarita glasses and enjoy!

description: Making a big batch of freezer margaritas couldn't be easier with this recipe. The margaritas are frozen right inside the tequila bottle.
directions: Pour 1 1/4 cups tequila from the bottle and reserve for another use.
Add lime juice, triple sec, agave, and salt to the bottle of tequila and place top on the bottle. Tilt bottle to lightly shake mixture until agave has been well incorporated.
Place bottle in the freezer for at least 8 hours or until ready to serve.

To serve, add 1 cup ice to a shaker or glass and top with about 1/2 cup of margarita mix. Shake mixture or pour margarita between two glasses until well blended and slightly frothy. Serve immediately with more ice if desired. This step is important to balance the taste through slight dilution from the ice.
ingredient_text: liter tequila bottle lime juice triple sec agave salt ice as needed

To serve, add 1 cup ice to a shaker or glass and top with about 1/2 cup of margarita mix. Shake mixture or pour margarita between two glasses until well blended and slightly frothy. Serve immediately with more ice if desired. This step is important to balance the taste through slight dilution from the ice.

description: You can use any type of jam for this jammy vodka drink. Also a great way to use up the last bits of jam in the glass - just use the jam glass as your shaker.

description: This guavarita, or guava margarita, made with guava nectar, tequila, and agave syrup, is simply delicious.
ingredients: 1 1/2 luid ounces orange liqueur, such as Cointreau®
directions: Pour salt onto a plate. Rub lime wedge around the rim of a glass, and dip the rim of the glass in the salt.

Fill a cocktail shaker with ice. Add guava nectar, tequila, Cointreau, lime juice, and agave syrup. Close the shaker and shake until the outside of the shaker is frosted, about 30 seconds. Pour into the salted glass.

description: This citrus adobo Margarita combines the bright flavors of fresh lime and orange juice with the smoky, spicy essence of adobo sauce. It is the perfect blend of zesty, spicy, and refreshing! For an extra smoky kick, use half silver tequila and half mezcal.
directions: Sprinkle chili lime seasoning onto a plate. Moisten the rim of a glass with a lime wedge. Press the moistened rim into the seasoning. Fill the glass with ice.
Fill a cocktail shaker with tequila, triple sec, lime juice, orange juice, adobo sauce, and agave syrup. Add 1 cup ice. Seal and shake vigorously until outside is frosted, 10 to 15 seconds.
Strain margarita into the glass and garnish with remaining lime wedge.

description: This spicy skinny Margarita is made with fresh orange juice and lime juice and sweetened with agave. I used habanero Tajin and floated jalapeno slices to make it even spicier.

Pour in tequila, lime juice, orange juice, and agave syrup; cover and shake until the outside of the shaker has frosted, about 30 seconds. Strain into a chilled glass and garnish with remaining jalapeno slices. Serve immediately.

description: This celery margarita is a fun and unexpected riff on a classic margarita using a homemade celery cordial. A cordial is a sweetened spirit or a nonalcoholic combination of sugar, water, spices, and fruits or veggies.
directions: Mix salt and coriander together on a small plate. Wipe rim of a margarita glass with lime wedge. Dip rim in salt mixture to coat. Fill glass with crushed ice.

Fill a cocktail shaker half full with ice. Add tequila, celery cordial, lime juice, and bitters to shaker; cover and shake until outside of shaker is frosted, about 60 seconds. Strain into prepared glass and garnish with celery stalk.

description: For this strawberry jalapeno margarita, you'll muddle fresh strawberries and jalapeno to spice up your margarita game! Garnish with a strawberry slice and enjoy the sweet heat of this delicious cocktail.
directions: Sprinkle salt onto a plate. Moisten the rim of a glass with lime wedge. Press the moistened rim into the salt. Fill the glass with ice.

Fill a cocktail shaker with jalapeno slices and strawberries, reserving 1 slice of strawberry for garnish. Use a muddler to crush the jalapeno and strawberries to release their juices. Add tequila, triple sec, lime juice and simple syrup to the shaker and add 1 cup of ice. Seal and shake vigorously until outside is frosted, 10 to 15 seconds.
Strain margarita into the glass and garnish with remaining strawberry slice, if desired.

Add tequila, triple sec, lime juice and simple syrup to the shaker and add 1 cup of ice. Seal and shake vigorously until outside is frosted, 10 to 15 seconds.
Strain margarita into the glass and garnish with remaining strawberry slice, if desired.

directions: Fill a cocktail shaker with ice. Add tequila, lime juice, orange juice, cream of coconut, and coconut milk. Shake until tequila mixture is completely chilled and cream of coconut is incorporated, about 30 seconds. Pour into glass. Serve immediately.

Strain margarita into the glass and garnish with remaining jalapeno slice, if desired.

description: A frozen strawberry margarita is easy to make at home with fresh strawberries, lime juice, tequila, and triple sec blended with ice for a fruity and refreshing variation on a classic frozen margarita. Perfect for summer parties!
ingredients: 1 teaspoon white sugar, or to taste
directions: Gather the ingredients.
Place strawberries, tequila, lime juice, triple sec, and 1 teaspoon sugar in a blender; process until smooth.
Add ice cubes; blend on high until ice is crushed to your liking, about 15 seconds.
Rub lime wedge around the rim of a glass. Spread 1 teaspoon sugar onto a plate. Dip glass rim in sugar to coat; pour into a margarita glass and enjoy!

description: I created this margarita pitcher recipe after tweaking several other margarita recipes to get that classic taste in a pitcher-size for parties.
directions: Combine ice, tequila, triple sec, fresh lime juice, and sweetened lime juice in a large pitcher; stir.
Pour kosher salt onto a plate. Rub the rim of a glass with lime wedge. Dip the rim of the glass into salt; fill the glass with margarita mixture; repeat for each serving.

description: This apple cider margarita is a nice fall twist on traditional margaritas using apple cider, tequila, cinnamon whiskey, butterscotch schnapps, and lime.
ingredients: 1 cup ice cubes, or as needed
directions: Moisten the rim of two margarita glasses with honey. Stir cinnamon and sugar together on a plate. Lightly dip the moistened rim of each glass into cinnamon and sugar mixture.

Fill a cocktail shaker with fresh ice. Add apple cider, tequila, cinnamon whiskey, and butterscotch schnapps. Squeeze 2 limes into the shaker. Cover and shake vigorously until the outside of the shaker has frosted.
Fill 2 glasses with ice; strain mixture into glasses. Garnish with apple slices.

1 thin slice jalapeño slice, plus more for garnish
directions: Puree watermelon in a blender. Wipe the rim of a margarita glass with a lime wedge; dip rim in salt.

description: This quick mango margarita recipe is easy to make by blending mango nectar with tequila, lime juice, and ice for an incredibly refreshing and fruity summer cocktail.
ingredients: 1 tablespoon white sugar, or as needed (Optional)
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Combine ice, margarita mix, tequila, and mango nectar in a blender; squeeze ½ lime into blender. Blend until smooth.
Cut remaining ½ lime into wedges. Pour ¼ to ½ inch sugar onto a small, shallow plate. Moisten rims of 4 glasses with 1 lime wedge; dip moistened glass rims into sugar.
Divide margarita among sugar-rimmed glasses; garnish with lime wedges.

ingredients: ¾ fluid ounce triple sec (orange-flavored liqueur)

Strain margarita into the glass and top with kombucha. Stir gently and garnish with lime wedge, if desired.

ingredients: ¾ fluid ounce freshly squeezed lime juice
1 tablespoon coarse salt, or as needed
directions: Fill a cocktail shaker half-full with ice. Add pineapple juice, tequila, triple sec, and lime juice to the shaker. Seal and shake vigorously until outside is frosted, 10 to 15 seconds.
Sprinkle salt onto a plate. Moisten the rim of a glass with a lime wedge. Press the moistened rim into the salt. Fill glass with ice.
Strain margarita into the glass and garnish with pineapple and lime wedge, if desired.

description: Have you ever had a tamarind margarita? This twist on the classic margarita has the perfect sweet-tart flavor from the tamarind paste. Add an extra 1/2 oz agave nectar if you like things a little sweeter.
directions: Sprinkle chili-lime seasoning onto a plate. Moisten the rim of a glass with the lime wedge. Press the moistened rim into the seasoning. Fill glass with ice.

Fill a cocktail shaker half-full with ice. Add tamarind paste, tequila, agave nectar, lime juice and triple sec to the shaker. Seal and shake vigorously until outside is frosted, 10 to 15 seconds. Strain mixture into the glass. Garnish with the lime wedge.

description: For this blackberry margarita, fresh blackberries are muddled to release their vibrant juices, then combined with smooth tequila, orange liqueur, simple syrup and a splash of lime juice, making this margarita both bold and refreshing.
directions: Sprinkle salt onto a plate. Moisten the rim of a glass with lime wedge. Press the moistened rim into the salt. Fill the glass with ice.

Fill a cocktail shaker with 10 blackberries, reserving 2 blackberries for garnish. Use a muddler to crush berries to release their juices. Add tequila, triple sec, simple syrup and lime juice to the shaker and add 1 cup of ice. Seal and shake vigorously until outside is frosted, 10 to 15 seconds.
Strain margarita into the glass and garnish with remaining blackberries.
ingredient_text: salt lime wedge ice blackberries fluid ounces tequila fluid ounce triple sec fluid ounce simple syrup fluid ounces lime juice

Fill a cocktail shaker with 10 blackberries, reserving 2 blackberries for garnish. Use a muddler to crush berries to release their juices. Add tequila, triple sec, simple syrup and lime juice to the shaker and add 1 cup of ice. Seal and shake vigorously until outside is frosted, 10 to 15 seconds.
Strain margarita into the glass and garnish with remaining blackberries.

description: A classic margarita cocktail drink served straight up or on the rocks, is one of the most popular cocktails of all time. And for good reason! It will cool you down on a hot day or warm you up on a cool day. Any day is a good day for a margarita.
ingredients: 1 fluid ounce orange flavored liqueur (such as Cointreau®)
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Sprinkle salt on a small plate. Lightly wet the rim of a cocktail glass or margarita glass with a damp paper towel. Dip the moistened rim in salt to coat. Set aside.

Combine tequila, orange-flavored brandy, and lime juice in a cocktail shaker. Add ice and shake until chilled. Strain into a salt-rimmed cocktail glass or a salt-rimmed, ice-filled margarita glass. Garnish with a lime wheel.
ingredient_text: kosher salt ½ fluid ounces tequila fluid ounce orange flavored liqueur such as cointreau ½ fluid ounce lime juice ice lime wheel

description: I first had a prickly pear margarita in Tucson and talked the bartender into sharing the recipe with me!
ingredients: 2 fluid ounces sweet and sour mix
directions: Pour salt onto a small plate. Wet the rim of a margarita glass and dip rim into salt.
Fill a cocktail shaker with ice; pour tequila, sweet and sour mix, triple sec, lime juice, and prickly pear syrup over ice. Cover shaker and shake vigorously; strain into prepared margarita glass.

description: This jalapeño margarita is refreshing and delicious. Agave nectar and fresh lime juice make a big difference. Include the jalapeño seeds for an extra kick!
ingredients: ½ jalapeno pepper, seeded and diced
directions: Pour 1/4 to 1/2 inch of salt onto a small, shallow plate. Moisten the rim of 2 margarita glasses with water and dip into salt. Fill with ice and set aside.
Fill a shaker with ice. Pour tequila, triple sec, lime juice, agave nectar, and jalapeño into the shaker. Cover and shake vigorously until the outside of the shaker has frosted.
Strain into the prepared glasses. Garnish with lime wedges to serve.

description: Enjoy this keto margarita without feeling guilty! This recipe uses Swerve instead of sugar and orange-flavored sparkling water to mimic the taste of orange liqueur.
ingredients: 2 ½ teaspoons low-calorie natural sweetener (such as Swerve®)
2 fluid ounces orange-flavored sparkling water (such as La Croix®)
directions: Fill a shaker half-full with ice. Add tequila, lime juice, and sweetener to the shaker. Seal and shake vigorously until outside is frosted, 10 to 15 seconds.
Place salt on a plate. Run 1 lime wedge along the rim of the Mason jar. Press the rim down into salt. Fill the jar with ice cubes.
Strain margarita into the jar. Top with sparkling water; stir. Garnish with remaining lime wedge.

description: It's beerita time! Who would believe that beer would be the perfect solution to eradicating fluorescent green margaritas? Well, it is. Best to use not-so-micro brews to avoid an overpowering beer flavor. Use the limeade can to measure the ingredients, and adjust with extra water if the mixture seems too sweet. Straining the pulp is always a good idea unless, of course, you like pulp.
ingredients: 1 (12 fluid ounce) can frozen limeade concentrate
12 fluid ounces water, or more as needed
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Pour limeade concentrate, tequila, water, and beer into a large pitcher. Stir until limeade has melted.
Add plenty of ice to the pitcher and garnish with lime wedges. Adjust with additional water if needed.

description: This cucumber jalapeño margarita recipe is so refreshing! Nothing tastes better than the combination of tequila and jalapeño — the cucumber just adds a freshness and helps to balance the heat. The longer it chills, the more jalapeño and cucumber infuse into the tequila. Serve over ice in a salt-rimmed glass, garnished with cucumber slices.
ingredients: ½ cup tequila, or more to taste
1 jalapeño chile pepper, halved and seeded
4 thin slices cucumber, or more to taste
2 tablespoons kosher salt, or as needed
directions: Combine tequila, lime juice, orange liqueur, and simple syrup in a pitcher with a lid; add jalapeño and 4 thin cucumber slices. Refrigerate until chilled, at least 1 hour.
Meanwhile, run lime wedges along rims of 4 pint glass; pour salt onto a flat plate. Dip moistened rims into salt to coat.
Fill prepared glasses with ice; pour chilled margarita over top. Garnish each drink with 1 cucumber slice.

description: This blended margarita recipe proves you don't need to purchase a pre-made margarita mix to make a perfect margarita. I prefer mine straight up, but you can add salt to the rim if you like.
ingredients: 1 cup triple sec or other orange liqueur
directions: Combine 2 cups ice cubes, lime juice, triple sec, tequila, and lemon juice in a blender; blend margarita until smooth.
Place remaining 1 cup ice cubes in a cocktail shaker; pour in desired amount of margarita. Cover and shake until the outside of the shaker has frosted. Strain into a chilled glass.

description: This presidente margarita requires good tequila and brandy. If you love a famous Mexican food chain's presidential margarita, you'll love this recipe.
ingredients: ½ fluid ounce orange-flavored liqueur (such as Cointreau)
½ fluid ounce brandy (such as Presidente)
directions: Pour 1/4 to 1/2 inch of salt onto a small, shallow plate. Moisten the rim of a margarita glass with 1 lime wedge; dip the moistened rim into salt. Fill the glass with ice cubes.
Pour cocktail mix, tequila, orange liqueur, and brandy into a cocktail shaker. Squeeze in juice from remaining lime wedge. Cover and shake to combine. Pour into the prepared glass.

description: Make a frozen margarita in a flash using frozen limeade. No need to squeeze lots of fresh limes!
ingredients: 1 (12 fluid ounce) can frozen limeade concentrate
directions: Working in 2 batches, place 1 quart ice cubes in a blender. Add 1/2 can limeade, 6 ounces tequila and 2 tablespoons Cointreau. Blend at highest speed until drink is almost smooth. Pour into a pitcher and repeat with remaining ingredients. Store in the freezer.

To salt the margarita glasses, pour a single layer of salt on a small plate. Moisten each glass rim with lime; dip each rim in the salt. Pour margaritas into each glass; garnish with lime. Serve immediately.
ingredient_text: quarts ice cubes fluid ounce can frozen limeade concentrate fluid ounces tequila ¼ cointreau

To salt the margarita glasses, pour a single layer of salt on a small plate. Moisten each glass rim with lime; dip each rim in the salt. Pour margaritas into each glass; garnish with lime. Serve immediately.

description: The name says it all--quality ingredients yield the best margarita!
ingredients: ½ fluid ounce Gran Gala Triple Orange Liqueur
1 fluid ounce agave nectar or simple syrup
directions: Shake together all ingredients with ice and strain into a margarita glass.
Optional: rub the cut side of a lime on the glass rim and dip it into salt; and add a lime wedge to the rim of the glass as a garnish.

description: I had to make hundreds of Jell-O® shots for a wedding, so I decided to have fun with them and try out some new mixes. This was my margarita-flavored shot, a bit strong but good if you want variety with your selection. If you want them more palatable, substitute water for a bit of the alcohol.
ingredients: 1 (3 ounce) package lime-flavored gelatin mix (such as Jell-O®)
15 plastic shot glasses, or as needed
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Combine boiling water and gelatin mix in a bowl. Stir to dissolve.
Add tequila, triple sec, and water. Stir again.
Pour mixture into shot glasses. Refrigerate until set, about 5 hours.
ingredient_text: boiling water ounce package lime-flavored gelatin mix such as jell-o ⅓ tequila ⅓ triple sec ⅓ water plastic shot glasses as needed
directions_text: gather all ingredients. combine boiling water and gelatin mix in a bowl. stir to dissolve. add tequila, triple sec, and water. stir again. pour mixture into shot glasses. refrigerate until set, about 5 hours.

description: This creamy coconut margarita is a delicious cross between a margarita and a pina colada! Great for Cinco de Mayo.
ingredients: 1 (15 ounce) can cream of coconut (such as Coco Lopez)
¾ cup tequila (such as Sauza 100% Blue Agave)
½ cup freshly squeezed lime juice
¼ cup brandy-based orange liqueur (such as Grand Marnier)
directions: Blend cream of coconut, ice, tequila, lime juice, and orange liqueur in a blender until smooth.
ingredient_text: ounce can cream coconut such as coco lopez ice ¾ tequila such as sauza blue agave ½ freshly squeezed lime juice ¼ brandy-based orange liqueur such as grand marnier

description: This fresh watermelon margarita tastes amazing and is so refreshing on hot summer days. You can serve this fruity margarita on the rocks or frozen. This recipe includes a simple syrup which you can make ahead and store in the refrigerator.
ingredients: 1 pinch salt or sugar for rimming glasses
2 cups crushed ice, or as needed
directions: To make a simple syrup: Bring 1/2 cup sugar, water, and orange zest in a small saucepan to boil, stirring constantly. Simmer until sugar is dissolved, about 3 minutes. Remove simple syrup from heat and allow to cool completely.
Place watermelon in a blender or food processor; blend until pureed.
Stir watermelon puree into a large pitcher with prepared simple syrup, tequila, and lime juice.

Place a small amount of salt or sugar into a saucer. Rub edge of margarita glasses with a lime wedge to moisten. Lightly dip the rim of the glass into the saucer to rim the glass; tap off excess salt or sugar.
Fill rimmed glasses with crushed ice; pour margarita mixture into glasses and garnish with lime wedges to serve.

description: An unusual twist to a classic drink! Watch out, these will sneak up on you!
directions: Sugar the rims of 4 large margarita glasses. To do so, pour sugar onto a small plate, moisten the rims of the glasses on a damp towel and press them into the sugar.
In a blender, combine tequila, limeade, triple sec and ice. Blend until smooth. Add bananas and blend again until smooth. Pour into glasses and serve.

description: This is the signature drink at my in-law's home. My father-in-law developed a taste for margaritas made from scratch during the summer they spent in Zihuatanejo during the 1960s. After decades of tinkering he has arrived at this foolproof recipe for the ultimate Mexican cocktail.
directions: Measure the tequila, lime juice, sweetened lime juice and triple sec into a cocktail shaker and add a generous scoop of ice. Cover and shake until the shaker is frosty, about 30 seconds.
Rub a lime wedge around the rim of a margarita glass and dip in salt. Fill each glass with ice. Strain equal amounts of the cocktail into the glasses to serve. Garnish with a lime wedge.
ingredient_text: fluid ounces tequila fluid ounces fresh lime juice fluid ounce sweetened lime juice fluid ounces triple sec orange-flavored liqueur ice cubes lime cut into wedges rimming salt

description: This is a basic margarita recipe. You can add your favorite fresh fruits to flavor it. Fresh strawberry or mango are good choices. For a little something different, try granulated sugar rims on the glasses instead of margarita salt.
directions: Salt the rims of 2 large margarita glasses. To do so, pour salt onto a small plate, moisten the rims of the glasses on a damp towel and press them into the salt.
In a blender combine tequila, triple sec and limeade concentrate. Fill blender with ice cubes. Blend until smooth. Pour into glasses, garnish with slice of lime and serve.

description: These slushy margaritas are made in the freezer. No blending required! But they must be made ahead to allow time to freeze.
directions: Mix the water, tequila, limeade, and orange liqueur in a freezer-proof container with a lid; stir. Cover and store in freezer until it reaches a slush-like consistency, about 24 hours. Serve in glasses garnished with lime wedges.
ingredient_text: ½ water ½ tequila fluid ounce can frozen limeade ½ triple sec orange-flavored liqueur whole lime cut into wedges
directions_text: mix the water, tequila, limeade, and orange liqueur in a freezer-proof container with a lid; stir. cover and store in freezer until it reaches a slush-like consistency, about 24 hours. serve in glasses garnished with lime wedges.

description: Grapefruit and lime are the perfect refreshing combination for this margarita. Fresh juices taste so much better than store-bought.
directions: Stir together the grapefruit juice, lime juice, triple sec, tequila, and agave syrup in a pitcher, and mix with ice cubes. Strain the cocktail into 4 margarita glasses; garnish each cocktail with a few pomegranate seeds.
ingredient_text: fresh grapefruit juice fresh lime juice ½ triple sec ½ tequila agave syrup ice cubes pomegranate seeds

recipe_title: Top Shelf Margaritas on the Rocks
description: Top Shelf Margaritas sure to please the avid 'rita fanatic; as with anything, fresh ingredients are the key to a quality result, so we start with our own syrup and sour mix.
directions: Make a simple syrup by stirring together and bringing 2/3 cup sugar and 1/3 cup water to a boil until the sugar dissolves; set aside to cool.
Make the sour mix by briskly stirring together the 2 1/4 cups water, lemon juice, 1/4 cup sugar, and egg white; set aside.
Combine the ice cubes, tequila, Cointreau, lime juice, 1 ounce of the simple syrup, and 1/2 cup of the sour mix in a pitcher; stir vigorously to infuse the ice, 8 to 15 seconds.

Run a lime wedge along the rim of an 8-ounce glass. Spread the kosher salt onto a plate and dip the glass rims in the salt to coat; add ice if desired. Strain the mixture from the cocktail shaker into the glass to serve.

Make the sour mix by briskly stirring together the 2 1/4 cups water, lemon juice, 1/4 cup sugar, and egg white; set aside.
Combine the ice cubes, tequila, Cointreau, lime juice, 1 ounce of the simple syrup, and 1/2 cup of the sour mix in a pitcher; stir vigorously to infuse the ice, 8 to 15 seconds.

Run a lime wedge along the rim of an 8-ounce glass. Spread the kosher salt onto a plate and dip the glass rims in the salt to coat; add ice if desired. Strain the mixture from the cocktail shaker into the glass to serve.

description: If you're tired of mediocre margaritas made with stomach-upsetting sour mix, Joe's perfect anti-sour mix margarita recipe is for you. Try this perfect margarita on the rocks made with a homemade "anti-sour" mix.
ingredients: ¼ cup salt, or as needed
2 cups ice cubes, or as needed, divided
1 fluid ounce sweetened lime juice (such as Rose's)
directions: Pour salt onto a small, shallow plate. Moisten rim of a large glass with water; dip into salt. Fill glass with ice; set aside.
Fill a cocktail shaker with ice; add tequila, orange liqueur, and lime juice. Cover and shake until the outside of the shaker has frosted. Strain into the prepared glass; slowly add grapefruit soda.

description: This watermelon margaritas recipe makes the perfect summer drink. Excellent for the kids to enjoy when made without alcohol.
directions: Combine watermelon, ice, tequila, sugar, lime juice, vodka, and orange liqueur in a blender; blend until smooth.
ingredient_text: cubed seeded watermelon crushed ice ⅓ tequila ¼ white sugar ¼ fresh lime juice vodka orange liqueur

description: Mommy's lemonade (margaritas) recipe uses Minute Maid limeade for a quick and easy cocktail that always gets rave reviews. This is the limeade margarita recipe that I use for all of our get-togethers. Enjoy!
ingredients: 1 (12 fluid ounce) can frozen limeade concentrate (such as Minute Maid), thawed
12 fluid ounces tequila (such as Cuervo Especial)
6 fluid ounces orange-flavored liqueur (such as Grand Marnier)
salt for rimming glasses, if desired (Optional)
directions: Pour limeade concentrate into a pitcher; fill the can with cold water three times, pouring each into the pitcher. Fill the can with tequila; pour into the pitcher. Fill the can halfway with orange liqueur; pour into the pitcher. Stir well; chill, about 30 minutes.
Pour salt onto a small, shallow plate. Moisten rims of 12 glasses with 1 lime wedge; dip moistened glasses into salt.
Fill glasses with ice; pour in margarita. Garnish with lime wedges.

description: This blue lagoon margarita is an amazing summer drink. It's beautiful and delicious!
ingredients: 1 cup ice, or more if desired
directions: Place ice into a blender; pour in sour mix, pineapple juice, tequila, blue curaçao, and lime juice. Cover and blend until the mixture is smooth. Pour into chilled glasses.

description: These Sprite margaritas are the best I've ever tasted — they look, smell, and taste more "pure" than any others I've had. A successful and upscale Mexican cantina nearby makes them. I always assumed they were using all top-shelf ingredients until I found out their secret: lemon-lime soda! Now I'm able to recreate them at home.
ingredients: 1 pinch kosher salt for rimming glasses
¼ cup sweetened lime juice (such as Rose's®)
¼ cup lemon-lime soda, or to taste
directions: Rim 2 margarita glasses with salt, if desired, and fill with ice. Pour tequila, sweetened lime juice, triple sec, and lemon-lime soda into a shaker filled with ice, hold your hand firmly over the top of the shaker so it doesn't pop off from the carbonated soda. Shake vigorously. Pour into prepared margarita glasses, and serve.

description: What do you do when you find yourself with 5 pounds of kiwis? Make margaritas! With a zippy kick-in-the-pants, this is a great new way to enjoy an old favorite.
directions: Combine sugar, tequila, triple sec, kiwis, and lime juice in a blender. Fill with ice cubes; blend until smooth.
ingredient_text: ½ superfine sugar ⅓ gold tequila ⅓ triple sec large kiwis peeled fresh lime juice small ice cubes

description: We enjoyed banana margaritas while vacationing in Mazatlán, Mexico, and have been trying to duplicate them ever since. This recipe brings back great memories. Serve in salt or sugar-rimmed margarita glasses.
directions: Combine bananas, banana liqueur, tequila, triple sec, lemon juice, and lime juice in the container of a blender. Add ice cubes until the mixture reaches the 6-cup line. Cover and blend until smooth.
Pour into margarita glasses to serve.
ingredient_text: large bananas ¾ banana liqueur ½ tequila ¼ triple sec orange-flavored liqueur lemon juice lime juice ice cubes as needed
directions_text: combine bananas, banana liqueur, tequila, triple sec, lemon juice, and lime juice in the container of a blender. add ice cubes until the mixture reaches the 6-cup line. cover and blend until smooth. pour into margarita glasses to serve.

description: These lime margaritas are made with the whole lime, including the rinds. Choose thin-skinned limes with plenty of juice and your lime margaritas will be bright and flavorful — nothing reminiscent of a bottled mix! The lime rind adds beautiful color and a burst of tart, but not overly sweet, taste. You'll never make a margarita the old way again.
ingredients: ¼ cup orange-flavored liqueur (such as Cointreau)
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Cut limes into quarters; remove and discard the center part of the rinds. Blend until completely smooth, about 1 minute.
Transfer lime quarters to a blender with cold water, tequila, sugar, orange-flavored liqueur, and salt.
Blend until completely smooth, about 1 minute.
Strain mixture into a large measuring cup or small pitcher
Serve over ice and garnish with lime slices.
ingredient_text: medium thin-skinned limes ice cold water tequila ½ white sugar ¼ orange-flavored liqueur such as cointreau ¼ kosher salt ice as needed slices lime

description: This mangorita recipe makes a Mexican-inspired mango margarita.
ingredients: 1 (1.5 fluid ounce) jigger triple sec liqueur
1 (1.5 fluid ounce) jigger fresh lime juice
directions: In a blender, combine tequila, triple sec, lime juice, mango, and ice. Blend until ice is finely crushed. Sweeten with mango nectar to your liking.

description: This purple margarita is made with gold tequila and raspberry liqueur and shaken up with a sour mix.
ingredients: ½ cup sweet and sour mix
directions: Pour salt onto a small plate. Moisten the rim of a margarita glass on a damp towel and press into the salt.
In a cocktail mixer full of ice, combine tequila, sweet and sour mix, and raspberry liqueur. Shake vigorously and strain into the salt-rimmed glass.

description: This spicy mezcal margarita is made with smoky mezcal tequila flavored with jalapeño, orange juice, and lime for a delicious spicy margarita cocktail.
ingredients: 1 jalapeño pepper, seeded and roughly chopped
½ cup freshly squeezed orange juice
directions: Combine 1 tablespoon sugar with salt on a small shallow plate. Moisten rims of two 8-inch glasses with a wedge of lime, dip the moistened glass rims into sugar mixture. Fill the glasses with ice, if desired.

Place jalapeño and 1 1/2 teaspoons sugar in a cocktail shaker and mash with a cocktail muddler until sugar dissolved and jalapeño juiced. Pour in orange juice, mezcal, and lime juice; fill with ice. Cover and shake until the outside of the shaker has frosted, about 30 seconds. Strain into the prepared glasses.

directions: Fill a cocktail shaker with ice; add gin, Cointreau, and lime juice. Shake in bitters. Shake well and strain into a chilled glass. Garnish with a slice of lime.

description: This rhubarb margarita is a sweet-tart combo that is fabulous on a hot day!
directions: Bring rhubarb and water to a simmer in a saucepan, covered, over medium heat. Reduce heat to medium-low; continue simmering until rhubarb breaks down and releases its juices, about 15 minutes.

Strain rhubarb mixture through a fine-mesh sieve, pressing pulp to squeeze out as much juice as possible. Discard pulp. Stir sugar into hot juice. Refrigerate rhubarb syrup until very cold, at least 2 hours.
Place ice into a blender; pour in rhubarb syrup and tequila. Cover and blend until mixture is smooth or until your desired consistency. Pour into chilled margarita glasses.

description: This is my version of an Italian amaretto margarita.
directions: Slightly moisten rims of four 12-ounce glasses and dip in confectioners' sugar to rim the glasses; fill each with crushed ice.
Combine sweet and sour mix, tequila, amaretto, and orange liqueur in a pitcher; stir. Pour mixture into the prepared glasses. Garnish each drink with orange and lime slices.

description: After living in Austin Texas for 10 years, my husband and I perfected the perfect margarita. The secret to success is fresh fruit juice only and it doesn't use any pre-made mixes! Don't skimp and try buying juice. Squeeze it yourself or you won't get the best taste! If you are a real olive lover, add pimento stuffed olives and call it a 'Mexican martini'...delicious! and you won't confuse yours with anyone else's if you add 3 olives on a stirrer. :)
ingredients: 2 (1.5 fluid ounce) jiggers freshly squeezed lime juice
1 (1.5 fluid ounce) jigger freshly squeezed orange juice
1 (1.5 fluid ounce) jigger freshly squeezed lemon juice
directions: Place the ice cubes into a margarita glass. Pour in the tequila, Cointreau, and Grand Marnier. Pour in the lime juice, orange juice, and lemon juice to taste. Garnish with a pimento stuffed olive if desired.

description: This golden margarita recipe is perfect — made with all fresh ingredients! I love margaritas. Let me rephrase that, I love good margaritas. This recipe took me about six months to perfect, and five years to share. I promise once you taste the freshness and balance of this recipe, you'll not only impress yourself, but your friends, too. This recipe is consistent, and will come out the same exact way every time.
ingredients: 2 (1.5 fluid ounce) jiggers reposado tequila (such as Jose Cuervo® Gold)
1 cup ice cubes, or as needed
1 teaspoon brandy-based orange liqueur (such as Grand Marnier®), or to taste
directions: Squeeze lime juice and lemon juice into a cocktail shaker; top with sugar. Pour tequila over sugar mixture and add ice. Cover shaker and shake. Allow shaker to rest for 1 to 2 minutes.
Spread salt onto a plate. Rub the lemon around the rim of the margarita glass and dip the rim into the salt to coat. Pour margarita into glass and top with a brandy-based orange liqueur.

description: This pineapple limoncello spritzer, with fresh basil and a splash of prosecco, will have your guests wanting more. Add a splash to your summer cocktails with this light and refreshing cocktail.
directions: Place limoncello, basil, and agave syrup into a cocktail shaker and mash well with a cocktail muddler. Pour in pineapple juice and ice, cover, and shake until the outside of the shaker has frosted. Strain into 2 chilled martini or champagne glasses, filling glasses halfway up.
Top glasses off with prosecco and garnish with a pineapple wedge or lemon wedge.
