description: You'll love this green apple martini if green apples are your thing. It's a very simple, sour, and delicious version of this traditional cocktail.
ingredients: 2 fluid ounces green apple pucker liqueur
1 tablespoon agave syrup or simple syrup
directions: Add green apple vodka, green apple pucker, lemonade, and agave syrup to a cocktail shaker. Fill the shaker with ice.
Shake very well and pour into 2 frosted martini glasses. Garnish with green apple slices.

description: Dolly Parton and Rachel Parton George share their unique twist on a classic martini in their cookbook "Good Lookin' Cookin'." In addition to olives, the sisters add in iced tea and pickled vegetables.
ingredients: 1/4 ounce brine from Mezzetta Italian Mix Giardiniera
vegetable pieces from Mezzetta Italian Mix Giardiniera, for garnish
directions: Combine vodka, tea, and brine in an ice-filled mixing glass; stir well to chill.
Fill a lowball or rocks glass with ice. Strain martini over ice. Garnish with olives and vegetable pieces.

recipe_title: Big Batch Freezer Lemon Drop Martinis
description: These big batch freezer lemon drop martinis, easily made right in a half-empty vodka bottle, are ready when you are—stash the bottle in the freezer and serve them up any time.
directions: Pour vodka, lemon juice, orange liqueur, and simple syrup into a freezer-safe 750ml bottle. Cover tightly, and tilt gently a few times to incorporate ingredients. Place bottle in the freezer for several hours until very well chilled.
Shake gently before serving. Serve as desired, as in a sugar-rimmed glass. Store any extra in the freezer until ready to use.
ingredient_text: vodka freshly squeezed lemon juice orange liqueur simple syrup
directions_text: pour vodka, lemon juice, orange liqueur, and simple syrup into a freezer-safe 750ml bottle. cover tightly, and tilt gently a few times to incorporate ingredients. place bottle in the freezer for several hours until very well chilled. shake gently before serving. serve as desired, as in a sugar-rimmed glass. store any extra in the freezer until ready to use.

description: This whole Meyer lemon drop will be the best lemon drop you've ever had. Blitz a whole Meyer lemon, vodka, ice, and a little sugar in the blender, strain, and go.
directions: Cut off the ends of the lemon. Use cut pieces to run around the rim of a martini glass. Dip glass in sugar to create a sugar rim.
Cut remaining lemon in quarters and cut each quarter in half. Place lemon pieces in blender. Add remaining ingredients and blend 30 seconds or until mostly smooth and frothy.
Strain mixture into sugar rimmed glass and serve immediately.

description: Named for Maryland's state flower, this high-octane sipper is the official cocktail of the Preakness Stakes. Shake up a few to serve at your watch party­—or any spring occasion that calls for a bright, punchy beverage.
directions: Fill a cocktail shaker half full with ice. Add 2 ounces orange juice and 1 ounce each lemon juice, simple syrup, bourbon, vodka, and schnapps. Cover and shake until outside of shaker is frosted, about 30 seconds.
Strain into an ice-filled highball glass. Repeat with remaining ingredients. Garnish each glass with orange slices and a cherry.

description: This watermelon sour patch is a surprise—it uses strawberry schnapps, but with the added vanilla vodka and sweet and sour mix it takes just like watermelon sour patch candy.
ingredients: 2 fluid ounces sweet and sour mix
directions: Pour 1/4 to 1/2 inch of sugar onto a small, shallow plate. Moisten rim of a rocks glass with a wedge of lime, dip the moistened glass into sugar; set aside.
Scoop ice into a shaker. Pour in strawberry schnapps, vanilla vodka, and sweet and sour mix; cover and shake until the outside is frosted. Strain into a chilled glass.

description: This fresh peaches and cream cocktail screams summer. Peaches, peach schnapps, and vodka combine in this simple yet tasty frozen drink.
directions: Place ice in a blender and crush for 15 to 20 seconds. Add fresh peaches, peach schnapps, and whipped vodka. Blend until smooth.
Rim glass with frozen whipped topping. Pour cocktail into glass and serve garnished with peach slice.

description: Channel your inner mixologist with infused vodka and homemade sour mix, then shake them together for a sweet-tart sipper you can call your own.
ingredients: 1/2 cup basil leaves, plus more for garnish
directions: For pineapple-basil vodka, put pineapple in a 1-quart jar. Top with vodka, cover, and let stand until vodka is infused with pineapple flavor, 1 to 3 days. Add basil, cover, and infuse for 1 to 2 hours; strain.
In a pitcher, stir together pineapple-basil vodka and DIY sour mix. Serve in rocks glasses over ice. Top with club soda (if using); garnish with pineapple wedges, fronds, and additional basil.

description: The Chilton cocktail, said to have originated in Lubbock, Texas, is a tangy and refreshing summer cocktail, perfect for those who don't like overly sweet drinks. Adjust the lemon and sparkling water to suit your taste.
ingredients: 1 ½ fluid ounces freshly-squeezed lemon juice
directions: Sprinkle salt onto a plate. Moisten the rim of a highball glass with lemon wedge. Press the moistened rim into salt. Fill the glass with ice.
Pour lemon juice and vodka over ice and gently stir. Top with sparkling water and serve with lemon wedge.
ingredient_text: salt as needed lemon wedge ½ fluid ounces freshly-squeezed lemon juice ½ fluid ounces vodka fluid ounces sparkling mineral water

description: This tropical vodka-based cocktail packs a flavorful punch, thanks to the star ingredient, matcha powder. Matcha, which is made from ground green tea leaves, tastes slightly grassy and bitter (in the very best way!) but when paired with pineapple and lime juice, it makes for a refreshing fruity drink. I suggest using ceremonial grade matcha for the best color and flavor pay off.
directions: Pour vodka, pineapple juice, lime juice, and matcha powder into a cocktail shaker.
Add ice, cover and shake until chilled.
Strain into a coupe or rocks glass. Garnish with mint and lime. Serve immediately.

description: A Bloody Mary is easy to make from scratch with vodka, tomato juice, and a few other simple ingredients. The spicy, salty, and savory taste of this classic cocktail makes it perfect for brunch or other afternoon gatherings.
ingredients: 1 teaspoon sea salt, or as needed
¾ cup spicy tomato-vegetable juice cocktail (such as V8®)
1 dash hot pepper sauce (such as Tabasco®)
salt and ground black pepper to taste
2 garlic-stuffed green olives, threaded onto a toothpick
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Pour salt onto a small plate. Moisten the rim of a glass and press into the salt. Fill the glass with ice cubes.

Fill a cocktail shaker with ice cubes; add vegetable juice cocktail, vodka, Worcestershire sauce, hot pepper sauce, salt, and pepper. Cover and shake until the outside of shaker has frosted, about 20 seconds.
Strain Bloody Mary into the prepared glass. Garnish with celery stalk and olives.
Enjoy!

description: Ice-cold limoncello is a distinguished, refreshing drink and worth the extra time it takes to make.
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Zest lemons, and place zest into a large glass bottle or jar.
Pour vodka into the bottle. Cover loosely and let infuse for 1 week at room temperature.
After 1 week, combine sugar and water in a medium saucepan and bring to a boil; do not stir. Boil for 15 minutes, then remove from the heat and allow syrup to cool to room temperature.
Stir vodka mixture into the syrup.
Strain into glass bottles, then seal each bottle with a cork. Let age at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Store bottled limoncello in the freezer until icy cold. Serve in chilled vodka glasses or shot glasses.

description: This apple pie moonshine tastes like apple pie in a jar, but it does pack quite a kick if you're not careful. The longer it sits in the fridge, the better it gets. Place a cinnamon stick in each jar if you like a lot of cinnamon. Makes a great gift.
ingredients: 4 cinnamon sticks, or more to taste
directions: Gather all ingredients.

Combine apple juice, apple cider, white sugar, brown sugar, cinnamon sticks, and apple pie spice in a large pot; bring to almost a boil. Cover the pot with a lid, reduce heat, and simmer for about 1 hour. Remove from heat and cool completely.
Stir grain alcohol and vodka into apple syrup; remove cinnamon sticks.
Pour apple pie moonshine into clean glass jars or bottles. Store in the refrigerator.

description: This delish apple pie moonshine made with apple juice, apple cider, brown sugar, cinnamon, and vanilla vodka tastes just like apple pie à la mode!
ingredients: 3 cups 190 proof grain alcohol (such as Everclear)
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Place apple cider, apple juice, white sugar, brown sugar, cinnamon sticks, and whole clove in a large pot over high heat. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to medium-low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Remove from heat and allow to cool completely. Stir grain alcohol and vanilla vodka into the cooled mixture.
Pour into clean bottles or jars; store in the refrigerator.
Enjoy!

description: A classic sidecar calls for Cognac, orange liqueur, and lemon juice, but this PB&J-inspired rendition subs in peanut butter whiskey and adds raspberry liqueur. Top it off with sparkling water for an effervescent cocktail with a hint of nostalgia.
directions: Spread sugar on a small plate. Rub rims of 2 cocktail glasses with lemon wedge. Dip rims in sugar to coat.

Fill a cocktail shaker half full with ice. Add whiskey, lemon juice, orange liqueur, and raspberry liqueur. Cover and shake until outside of shaker is frosted, about 60 seconds. Strain into prepared glasses. Top each cocktail with 1 1/2 ounces sparkling water and garnish with a raspberry.

description: The old fashioned is a classic cocktail that was invented in Louisville, KY. You can make it with bourbon, rye, or a blended whiskey. You can also substitute one sugar cube for the simple syrup.
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Pour simple syrup, water, and bitters into a whiskey glass; stir to combine.
Add ice cubes and pour in bourbon.
Garnish with orange slice and maraschino cherry.
Enjoy!

Pour boiling water, whiskey, and honey into a mug.
Add cloves, cinnamon, and lemon slice. Let mixture stand for 5 minutes so flavors can mingle, then sprinkle with a pinch of nutmeg before serving.

description: The mint julep cocktail is made with bourbon and sweetened with simple syrup. I like to use a minty homemade simple syrup which you can make ahead with this recipe and store in the refrigerator. This refreshing cocktail deserves to be served in frozen silver goblets or the most elegant glasses you have — perfect for Kentucky Derby viewing!
ingredients: ½ cup roughly chopped fresh mint leaves
directions: Gather the ingredients.
Make the simple syrup ahead of when you plan to serve: Bring water, sugar, and chopped mint to a boil over high heat in a small saucepan.
Cook, stirring occasionally, until sugar is completely dissolved, 1 to 2 minutes. Remove from the heat and let cool .
Pour syrup through a strainer to remove mint leaves.
To make the mint juleps: Fill eight glasses with crushed ice; pour 4 ounces bourbon and 1/4 cup mint syrup into each cup.
Top each drink with a mint sprig.

description: Balanced with orange and a light touch of spice, this punch is whiskey-forward without being overpowering. For a nonalcoholic version, omit the whiskey and add a bigger splash of club soda.
directions: Bring water to a boil in a saucepan over high heat. Stir in sugar, cinnamon stick, and star anise. Remove from heat and add tea bags. Let steep for 3 minutes (or according to package directions for strongest tea). Remove tea bags. Let cool to room temperature, about 1 hour. Remove and discard cinnamon stick and star anise.
Stir together tea mixture, orange juice, whiskey, and lemon juice in a punch bowl or pitcher. Chill until cold, about 2 hours or up to overnight.
Garnish with tangerine slices and additional star anise. Ladle into ice-filled glasses. Top each with a tangerine slice.

description: Homemade Irish cream liqueur made with Irish whiskey, cream, and sugar with hints of coffee, chocolate, vanilla, and almond. Delicious served on the rocks for a rich and creamy holiday treat! Will keep for 2 months if refrigerated.
directions: Gather all ingredients.

Combine sweetened condensed milk, Irish whiskey, heavy cream, chocolate syrup, coffee granules, vanilla, and almond extract in a blender. Blend on high speed until incorporated, 20 to 30 seconds. Store in a tightly sealed container in the refrigerator.
Shake well before serving.

description: An easy recipe for a Manhattan — a classic cocktail that is believed to date back to the late 1800s. It's made with a bracing mixture of bourbon or rye whiskey mixed with sweet vermouth and a dash of aromatic bitters.
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Combine whiskey, vermouth, and bitters in a cocktail mixing glass. Add ice and stir until chilled.
Strain into a chilled cocktail glass and garnish with a maraschino cherry.
Enjoy!

description: Glögg is a Swedish punch made from spiced wine. My grandfather brought this recipe over from Sweden in 1921. We still use it today. "God Jul!"
directions: Heat port wine in a large stockpot with a lid over medium heat until just below the simmering point. Add bourbon and rum; heat until just below simmering. Save the bottles and their caps for storing leftover glögg.
Meanwhile, place cinnamon stick, orange peel, cloves, and cardamom pods in the center of the cheesecloth square. Gather edges of the cheesecloth and tie with kitchen twine to secure; set aside.

When wine mixture is very hot but not boiling, carefully light it with a long-handled match. Wearing a heatproof cooking mitt, carefully pour sugar into the flames; let mixture burn for 1 minute. Place the lid on the stockpot to extinguish the flames and turn off the heat. Let mixture cool, covered, about 10 minutes.
Add the cheesecloth spice bundle, raisins, and almonds to warm wine mixture; let it cool to room temperature, about 1 hour.
Strain cooled glögg and reserve the raisins and almonds.

To serve: Pour glögg into a saucepan and warm over medium-low heat until hot but not simmering, about 5 minutes. Ladle 3 ounces of warmed glögg into a small coffee cup or a small Swedish-style glögg mug, and garnish each serving with a few reserved raisins and almonds.

description: This old fashioned cocktail recipe has a history. The earliest version of it was simply water, sugar, bitters, and booze. Over time, the water became ice, the booze became whiskey, and the drink became an old fashioned.
ingredients: ½ cup ice cubes, or as needed
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Muddle sugar cube, water, and bitters in an old fashioned glass for 1 minute. Pour in whiskey; stir for 1 minute.
Squeeze lemon twist over the glass and drop it in. Add ice cubes. Garnish with orange slice and maraschino cherry; serve with a swizzle stick.

description: A classic lemon whiskey sour should be made from scratch, or it ain't a whiskey sour. For a cleaner, classier-looking drink, strain the lemon juice to remove seeds and pulp. This cocktail can be served in martini glasses or tumblers full of ice.
ingredients: 1 cup ice cubes, or as needed
directions: Combine whiskey, lemon juice, and simple syrup in a shaker. Fill with ice. Cover and shake until the shaker is frosty, about 30 seconds.
Strain into 3 martini glasses and garnish each glass with a maraschino cherry.

description: The John Collins cocktail is similar to a Tom Collins but features bourbon whiskey instead of gin. It still mixes in club soda, simple syrup, and lemon juice to make a refreshing cocktail. Serve with a straw.
directions: Combine bourbon, lemon juice, and simple syrup in a cocktail shaker. Add 1 cup ice, cover, and shake until chilled. Strain into a Collins glass. Add 1 cup ice, fill with club soda, and stir.
Garnish with orange and lemon slices and a maraschino cherry.

description: Straightforward and timeless, this Maker's Mark old-fashioned cocktail never goes out of style. A simple combination of bourbon, bitters, sugar, and a splash of water — just garnish your masterpiece with an orange slice and a cherry on top and you've got the perfect drink. Serve it on poker night, game day, or enjoy as a perfect after-work drink.
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Muddle sugar, bitters, orange, cherry, and water in an old-fashioned glass.
Remove the orange rind and fill the glass 3/4 full of ice.
Add Maker's Mark Bourbon and stir.

description: This Southern Comfort recipe makes a cocktail that is extremely tasty. Once you try this Manhattan, you may need to go back to a cheaper whiskey. I make them shaken "up" on a cool day before dinner or with ice on a hot day. High-quality ingredients are the key to making this drink unforgettable. It's well worth it if you're over 45 and a Manhattan lover.
ingredients: 1 cup ice cubes, or as needed
2 (1.5 fluid ounce) jiggers Southern Comfort liqueur
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Fill a cocktail shaker with ice.
Pour in Southern Comfort, vermouth, and aromatic bitters.
Cover and shake until the outside of the shaker has frosted. Strain into a highball glass with or without ice, then garnish with maraschino cherry.

directions: Place 2 large ice cubes in a rocks class. Set aside in the freezer.

description: This bourbon slush recipe is a fruity blend of juices, tea, and whiskey frozen into a slush, then topped off with lemon-lime soda. This takes a while, but it's worth it!
ingredients: 1 (6 ounce) can frozen orange juice concentrate
1 (2 liter) bottle lemon-lime flavored carbonated beverage
directions: Stir pineapple juice, black tea, bourbon, sugar, lemonade concentrate, and orange juice concentrate together in a large bowl or container. Pour into shallow bowls or dishes. Freeze 8 hours to overnight.
Set slushies aside at room temperature for about 10 minutes; chop with a wire whisk or potato masher for a slushy consistency. Scoop into glasses; top with lemon-lime soda.

description: Follow this recipe to make the best Manhattan cocktail, then serve it straight up or on the rocks. Most Manhattan drinkers prefer this classic drink to be stirred, not shaken. (Only shake it when asked to do so!)
ingredients: 1 cup ice cubes, or as needed
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Fill a mixing glass with ice cubes. Pour in vermouth, then whiskey.
Stir, then strain into a cocktail glass.
Add bitters.
Garnish with cherry.

description: My best friend, who just happens to be Irish, made this hot Irish whiskey drink for me one cold night in Chicago, and since then, I have been hooked! Warning: It is very potent. Just one of these will warm you up and basically make you good for nothing afterward — what a treat! It is super to drink at night if you have a sore throat. My friend said this is what the Irish drink if they don't feel good, but don't wait until you have a cold to try this recipe!
directions: Press cloves into lemon slice peel around entire peel; set aside.

Add sugar to a wine glass; place a metal spoon into the glass with the curved side facing upwards. Pour boiling water over the back of the spoon; this will keep your wine glass from shattering. Stir until sugar dissolved; add whiskey and lemon slice. Steep before drinking, about 1 minute.

description: There's a lot to love about this whiskey and bitters beverage recipe. The official drink of New Orleans, the Sazerac cocktail, is every bit as aromatic and flavorful as most anything else from down on the bayou.
directions: Fill a rocks glass with 1 cup ice; set aside to chill, about 5 minutes. Discard ice.

Meanwhile, place Peychaud's bitters, Angostura bitters, and sugar cube in a cocktail shaker; mash well with a cocktail muddler. Add remaining 1 cup ice and whiskey; stir until chilled. Strain into the chilled glass.
Twist lemon peel over surface of cocktail to extract its essence; discard lemon peel.

Meanwhile, place Peychaud's bitters, Angostura bitters, and sugar cube in a cocktail shaker; mash well with a cocktail muddler. Add remaining 1 cup ice and whiskey; stir until chilled. Strain into the chilled glass.
Twist lemon peel over surface of cocktail to extract its essence; discard lemon peel.

description: Imagine a luxuriously textured bourbon-spiked custard being sipped through a thin layer of nutmeg-scented meringue. Based on Alton Brown's famous version, this eggnog is vastly superior to the store-bought product.
ingredients: 2 fluid ounces bourbon whiskey, or more to taste
directions: Place egg yolks in a saucepan. Whisk in sugar until well blended and creamy. Add milk and cream; whisk until blended. Place pan over medium heat; whisk frequently until mixture reaches a temperature of between 170 to 180 degrees F (75 to 80 degrees C) using an instant-read thermometer. Remove from heat. Stir in nutmeg and whiskey.
Chill pan in cold water until cool. Transfer mixture to a container (a large pitcher is ideal). Cover and refrigerate until thoroughly chilled, 2 to 3 hours.

Place egg whites in a bowl and whisk until soft peaks form. Add sugar and continue whisking until egg whites hold a firmer peak. Whisk egg whites into custard base until thoroughly blended. Refrigerate until chilled, about 30 minutes.
Whisk eggnog again just before serving and between each pour to ensure even distribution of the egg whites which tend to float to the top. Serve with a dusting of nutmeg.
ingredient_text: egg yolks ⅓ white sugar whole milk heavy cream ¾ freshly grated nutmeg fluid ounces bourbon whiskey more taste
directions_text: place egg yolks in a saucepan. whisk in sugar until well blended and creamy. add milk and cream; whisk until blended. place pan over medium heat; whisk frequently until mixture reaches a temperature of between 170 to 180 degrees f (75 to 80 degrees c) using an instant-read thermometer. remove from heat. stir in nutmeg and whiskey. chill pan in cold water until cool. transfer mixture to a container (a large pitcher is ideal). cover and refrigerate until thoroughly chilled, 2 to 3 hours. place egg whites in a bowl and whisk until soft peaks form. add sugar and continue whisking until egg whites hold a firmer peak. whisk egg whites into custard base until thoroughly blended. refrigerate until chilled, about 30 minutes. whisk eggnog again just before serving and between each pour to ensure even distribution of the egg whites which tend to float to the top. serve with a dusting of nutmeg.
combined_text: chef john's homemade eggnog cocktails whiskey drinks 4 egg yolks ⅓ cup white sugar 2 cups whole milk 1 cup heavy cream ¾ teaspoon freshly grated nutmeg 2 fluid ounces bourbon whiskey, or more to taste place egg yolks in a saucepan. whisk in sugar until well blended and creamy. add milk and cream; whisk until blended. place pan over medium heat; whisk frequently until mixture reaches a temperature of between 170 to 180 degrees f (75 to 80 degrees c) using an instant-read thermometer. remove from heat. stir in nutmeg and whiskey. chill pan in cold water until cool. transfer mixture to a container (a large pitcher is ideal). cover and refrigerate until thoroughly chilled, 2 to 3 hours. place egg whites in a bowl and whisk until soft peaks form. add sugar and continue whisking until egg whites hold a firmer peak. whisk egg whites into custard base until thoroughly blended. refrigerate until chilled, about 30 minutes. whisk eggnog again just before serving and between each pour to ensure even distribution of the egg whites which tend to float to the top. serve with a dusting of nutmeg.
ingredients_raw: 2 fluid ounces bourbon whiskey, or more to taste
directions_raw: Place egg yolks in a saucepan. Whisk in sugar until well blended and creamy. Add milk and cream; whisk until blended. Place pan over medium heat; whisk frequently until mixture reaches a temperature of between 170 to 180 degrees F (75 to 80 degrees C) using an instant-read thermometer. Remove from heat. Stir in nutmeg and whiskey.
Chill pan in cold water until cool. Transfer mixture to a container (a large pitcher is ideal). Cover and refrigerate until thoroughly chilled, 2 to 3 hours.

Place egg whites in a bowl and whisk until soft peaks form. Add sugar and continue whisking until egg whites hold a firmer peak. Whisk egg whites into custard base until thoroughly blended. Refrigerate until chilled, about 30 minutes.
Whisk eggnog again just before serving and between each pour to ensure even distribution of the egg whites which tend to float to the top. Serve with a dusting of nutmeg.
ingredients_canonical: fluid ounces bourbon whiskey more taste

description: An oldie but a goody, Sazerac is one of the world's first cocktails, invented in New Orleans in the 19th century.
directions: Place a cocktail glass in the freezer for 15 to 20 minutes before making the cocktail.
Pour anise-flavored liqueur into the frosted glass and turn to coat the sides. Dump excess liqueur in the sink (or drink it!).
Add sugar, water, and bitters to the glass, stirring to dissolve. Add ice cubes and rye whiskey. Stir and garnish with a twist of lemon.

description: Jack Daniel's eggnog for all seasons! Any brand of liquor is fine; put your spin on it by using your favorite. And remember, a little nutmeg goes a long way.
ingredients: 1 pinch ground nutmeg, or to taste
directions: Mix sugar, rum, bourbon, brandy, vanilla, nutmeg, and salt in a blender on medium speed until combined. Transfer to a large bowl.
Pour half-and-half, cream, and eggs into the blender; mix on low speed until combined. Carefully pour into the liquor mixture and stir to combine.
Ladle combined mixture back into the blender in batches; blend on low speed. Transfer blended eggnog to a punch bowl or pitcher.
Refrigerate eggnog until cold, 1 to 2 hours, before serving.

description: This is the best whiskey sour recipe. I'm excited to share my favorite formula for this incredibly refreshing, super simple, absolutely delicious drink. Day drinking is the best, and if that day happens to be sunny and hot, there's no better cocktail to enjoy than a classic whiskey sour. Garnish with a slice of blood orange and a Luxardo cherry.
directions: Prepare simple syrup: Bring sugar and water to a simmer in a small pan over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until sugar is dissolved. Pour into a small bowl and let cool completely before using, 15 to 20 minutes.

Make cocktail: Pour 3/4 fluid ounce simple syrup, bourbon, lemon juice, bitters, and egg white into a large cocktail shaker. Cover with a smaller cocktail shaker, or top, and give cocktail a "dry shake" without ice, about 30 seconds.

Add 3 ice cubes to bourbon mixture, cover, and shake vigorously until the shaker is very cold and the outside is frosty, about 45 seconds. Uncover and strain into a rocks glass containing 1 large ice cube.
ingredient_text: ½ white sugar ¼ water

Make cocktail: Pour 3/4 fluid ounce simple syrup, bourbon, lemon juice, bitters, and egg white into a large cocktail shaker. Cover with a smaller cocktail shaker, or top, and give cocktail a "dry shake" without ice, about 30 seconds.

Add 3 ice cubes to bourbon mixture, cover, and shake vigorously until the shaker is very cold and the outside is frosty, about 45 seconds. Uncover and strain into a rocks glass containing 1 large ice cube.

description: Strawberries and fresh basil are smashed together until fragrant in this bourbon-based cocktail perfect for summer.

description: I named this the Autumn Sunshine Cocktail because its flavor is bright like the sun, but the maple syrup, for some reason, reminds me of burning leaves in the autumn. It is definitely the color of autumn! For a clearer, more pungent cocktail, try stirring instead of shaking.
ingredients: 1 fluid ounce brandy-based orange liqueur (such as Grand Marnier)
1 clementine, juiced and peel cut into 1x1/4-inch strips
directions: Place ice cubes in a shaker. Pour in bourbon, maple syrup, orange liqueur, clementine juice, and lemon juice. Cover the shaker and shake until the outside of the shaker has frosted, about 15 seconds. Strain into a chilled martini glass.
Light a match or lighter over cocktail and squeeze 1 strip of clementine peel near the flame to flame the oil. Rub peel on the rim of the martini glass and drop peel into cocktail.

description: This classic Irish Coffee is the ultimate balance of strong, hot coffee, rich Irish whiskey, a touch of sweetness, and a luscious cream topping. This cozy cocktail is perfect for chilly evenings!
ingredients: 1 tablespoon white sugar, more as needed
directions: Place 1 tablespoon sugar on a plate or in a shallow bowl. Lightly wet the rim of an Irish coffee glass with a moistened paper towel. Dip the rim in sugar to coat.
Pour Irish whiskey into the prepared glass. Fill with coffee to within 1/2 inch of top. Add 1 teaspoon sugar and stir. Top with whipped cream.

description: This cherry smash recipe is a variation of a whiskey cocktail. It's a sipping drink and so good. Super swanky and very retro with a modern take. It's my new favorite cocktail.
ingredients: 2 fluid ounces rye whiskey (such as Michter's)
½ teaspoon lemon juice, or to taste
directions: Place cherries in an old-fashioned glass; splash with bitters. Mash cherries with a cocktail muddler until desired consistency.
Fill a cocktail shaker with ice; add rye, vermouth, simple syrup, and lemon juice. Cover and shake until the outside of the shaker has frosted. Strain cocktail into glass over cherries.

description: There's nothing like a warm and toasty hot toddy on a cold winter's day! Honey, sugar, and lemony goodness combine with the exquisite, smooth, full-bodied taste of Jim Beam Black to create a hot toddy recipe that is perfect for the holiday season.
directions: Put bourbon, lemon juice, and honey in a 6-ounce mug.
Top off with hot water and stir until honey is dissolved.
Garnish with a lemon peel (optional).

description: In this easy mint julep recipe, the whiskey varies by state: Kentucky uses bourbon, Georgia favors peach brandy, and Maryland loves rye. Take your pick!
ingredients: 5 fresh mint leaves, or to taste
1 (1.5 fluid ounce) jigger bourbon, or more to taste
directions: Place a tall glass in the freezer until frosted.

Stir water and sugar together in the prepared glass until sugar dissolved. Twist each mint leaf to release the scent and flavor; add to the glass. Pour bourbon into the glass, it should be about 1/4 full. Add ice and stir; garnish with a mint leaf.

description: A fun and tasty way of keeping your Jell-O shots Irish. Just add Jameson Irish whiskey and Baileys Strawberries Cream liqueur to your layered shot glasses.
ingredients: ½ (3 ounce) package lime-flavored gelatin mix (such as Jell-O®)
½ cup Irish whiskey (such as Jameson®)
1 (1 ounce) package unflavored gelatin (such as Knox®)
½ cup strawberry cream liqueur (such as Baileys®)
directions: Place shot glasses in a mini muffin tin at an angle.

Pour lime gelatin mix into a bowl. Add 1/2 cup boiling water and stir until dissolved. Add Irish whiskey and stir until well incorporated. Pour mixture carefully into each shot glass, filling to near the top of the slanted glasses. Refrigerate until set, about 4 hours.
Pour unflavored gelatin into a bowl. Add 1/2 cup boiling water and stir until dissolved. Let rest 3 to 5 minutes. Add strawberry cream liqueur and stir until well incorporated.
Remove glasses from muffin tin and place upright. Top with strawberry mixture. Return glasses to the refrigerator and chill until set, about 4 hours more.

description: This frozen mudslide recipe made with vodka, coffee liqueur, Irish cream, and ice is a rich and creamy cocktail. Serve in chocolate drizzled glasses with whipped cream and an extra little chocolate drizzle!
ingredients: 2 (1.5 fluid ounce) jiggers Irish cream liqueur
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Combine crushed ice, vodka, coffee liqueur, and Irish cream liqueur in a blender. Drizzle in chocolate syrup. Blend until smooth.
Pour into glasses and garnish with whipped cream.

description: The Blue Hawaiian is a fun, refreshing cocktail made with rum, cream of coconut, pineapple juice, and Curacao liqueur to create its legendary blue color. Invented in Hawaii in the 1950s, this tiki cocktail will have you crooning like Elvis!
directions: Combine crushed ice, pineapple juice, rum, blue Curacao, and cream of coconut in a blender.
Blend on high speed until smooth and an even blue color.
Pour into a chilled highball glass. Garnish with a slice of pineapple and a maraschino cherry.

description: This banana daiquiri recipe is a nice change of pace from the usual strawberry daiquiri.
ingredients: 1 teaspoon white sugar, or to taste
directions: Combine banana, rum, lime juice, triple sec, and sugar in a blender; purée until smooth.
Add ice cubes and blend on the highest setting until slushy, 15 to 20 seconds. Pour into a glass and serve.

description: This is the best batch of margaritas on the rocks you'll ever make! Everybody wants the recipe after enjoying one of these.
ingredients: 3 tablespoons kosher salt, or as needed
⅓ cup brandy-based orange liqueur (such as Grand Marnier)
directions: Gather all ingredients.
To salt the rims of your glasses: Pour salt onto a small plate, rub the rims of the glasses with lime, and press them into the salt.
Fill 8 glasses with ice.
Combine sweet and sour mix, tequila, triple sec, and orange liqueur in a blender or pitcher.
Pour into glasses, squeeze a quarter lime into each glass, and serve.

description: This Cuban mojito is very similar to the drinks I enjoyed in Varadero, Cuba. The muddling and the order of the ingredients helps ensure that all the flavors are well blended. Serve with a straw and a decorative stir stick to keep the different flavors well mixed together.
ingredients: 2 cups crushed ice, or as needed
directions: Place 1 teaspoon sugar each into two 12-ounce glasses. Squeeze juice from a lime wedge into each glass and drop in the wedge. Place 2 mint sprigs into each glass. Use a spoon or muddler to mash lime mixture together in the bottom of the glasses.
Fill each glass about half full with crushed ice. Pour 1/4 cup rum into each glass. Fill the glasses with club soda, stir, and garnish with remaining lime wedges.

description: This blended margarita recipe proves you don't need to purchase a pre-made margarita mix to make a perfect margarita. I prefer mine straight up, but you can add salt to the rim if you like.
ingredients: 1 cup triple sec or other orange liqueur
directions: Combine 2 cups ice cubes, lime juice, triple sec, tequila, and lemon juice in a blender; blend margarita until smooth.
Place remaining 1 cup ice cubes in a cocktail shaker; pour in desired amount of margarita. Cover and shake until the outside of the shaker has frosted. Strain into a chilled glass.

description: This is a favorite girl's-night drink for me. We love to mix these up any time of the year but even better on a hot summer day.
directions: Place thawed frozen strawberries, amaretto liqueur, vanilla ice cream, vanilla extract, crushed ice, and vanilla vodka into a blender.
Cover and blend until smooth.
Pour into a glass and garnish with whipped cream and a fresh strawberry.

description: This watermelon sangria is one of my favorite summer cocktail recipes. If you love watermelon and white wine, you'll love this fruity drink on a hot day!
directions: Place 9 cups of watermelon cubes in a blender; blend on high until very smooth. Strain juice through a mesh strainer into a large pitcher.

Pour wine, vodka, triple sec, and simple syrup into the pitcher; stir to combine. Add remaining 3 cups of watermelon cubes, orange sections, blueberries, and lime quarters; chill sangria for 4 hours before serving.

description: This is from a friend of mine. It is so good!
directions: In a food processor, puree the watermelon flesh. Pour the pureed watermelon into empty ice cube trays and freeze for at least 4 hours. Also, freeze 4 martini glasses.
In a blender combine the frozen watermelon cubes, simple syrup, lemon juice, vodka, and melon liqueur; blend until smooth. Pour into 4 frozen martini glasses and garnish each with a lemon twist.

description: Enjoy this classic daiquiri frozen or on the rocks.
ingredients: 2 tablespoons white sugar, or as needed
directions: Blend ice, rum, lime juice, triple sec, and 1 teaspoon sugar in a blender on the highest setting until smooth, 15 to 20 seconds. Pour into glass.
Spread 2 tablespoons sugar in a thin layer onto a small plate. Rub lime wedge around the rim of a glass. Dip glass rim in sugar to coat. Pour blended beverage into the prepared glass to serve.

description: A friend's mom made this on a hot summer day after she got back from a cruise. It's one of the best pina colada drinks I've ever had.
ingredients: ½ cup Irish cream liqueur (such as Baileys®)
½ cup coconut-flavored rum (such as Malibu®)
directions: Mix the Irish cream liqueur, rum, pineapple juice, and cream of coconut together in a large pitcher. Pour 1/2 cup into a blender container. Add the ice cubes and blend to desired consistency. Pour the ice mixture into two glasses, dividing equally, and fill with the remaining rum mixture.

directions: Combine red wine, ice cubes, blueberries, raspberries, strawberries, orange-flavored liqueur, orange juice, and sugar in a blender; process until smooth. Serve immediately or freeze.

description: The pina colada is a blended drink that originated in Puerto Rico. The mixture of pineapple, coconut, and rum is a taste of the tropics.
directions: Combine rum, cream of coconut, pineapple, and ice in a blender. Puree on high speed until smooth. Pour into chilled Collins glass and serve with a straw.
ingredient_text: fluid ounces light rum cream coconut crushed pineapple ice

description: This fresh watermelon margarita tastes amazing and is so refreshing on hot summer days. You can serve this fruity margarita on the rocks or frozen. This recipe includes a simple syrup which you can make ahead and store in the refrigerator.
ingredients: 1 pinch salt or sugar for rimming glasses
2 cups crushed ice, or as needed
directions: To make a simple syrup: Bring 1/2 cup sugar, water, and orange zest in a small saucepan to boil, stirring constantly. Simmer until sugar is dissolved, about 3 minutes. Remove simple syrup from heat and allow to cool completely.
Place watermelon in a blender or food processor; blend until pureed.
Stir watermelon puree into a large pitcher with prepared simple syrup, tequila, and lime juice.

Place a small amount of salt or sugar into a saucer. Rub edge of margarita glasses with a lime wedge to moisten. Lightly dip the rim of the glass into the saucer to rim the glass; tap off excess salt or sugar.
Fill rimmed glasses with crushed ice; pour margarita mixture into glasses and garnish with lime wedges to serve.

2 cups crushed ice, or as needed

description: An unusual twist to a classic drink! Watch out, these will sneak up on you!

In a blender, combine tequila, limeade, triple sec and ice. Blend until smooth. Add bananas and blend again until smooth. Pour into glasses and serve.

description: A strawberry bellini is a nice drink for Mother's Day, a brunch, or a shower.
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Blend hulled strawberries, confectioners' sugar, and brandy in a blender until smooth. Chill in the refrigerator for 10 minutes.
Divide the strawberry mixture among three champagne flutes.
Pour 1/2 cup sparkling wine into each flute and stir to combine.
Garnish each glass with a whole strawberry.

description: We enjoyed banana margaritas while vacationing in Mazatlán, Mexico, and have been trying to duplicate them ever since. This recipe brings back great memories. Serve in salt or sugar-rimmed margarita glasses.

Pour into margarita glasses to serve.

description: These lime margaritas are made with the whole lime, including the rinds. Choose thin-skinned limes with plenty of juice and your lime margaritas will be bright and flavorful — nothing reminiscent of a bottled mix! The lime rind adds beautiful color and a burst of tart, but not overly sweet, taste. You'll never make a margarita the old way again.
ingredients: ¼ cup orange-flavored liqueur (such as Cointreau)
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Cut limes into quarters; remove and discard the center part of the rinds. Blend until completely smooth, about 1 minute.
Transfer lime quarters to a blender with cold water, tequila, sugar, orange-flavored liqueur, and salt.
Blend until completely smooth, about 1 minute.
Strain mixture into a large measuring cup or small pitcher
Serve over ice and garnish with lime slices.
ingredient_text: medium thin-skinned limes ice cold water tequila ½ white sugar ¼ orange-flavored liqueur such as cointreau ¼ kosher salt ice as needed slices lime
directions_text: gather all ingredients. cut limes into quarters; remove and discard the center part of the rinds. blend until completely smooth, about 1 minute. transfer lime quarters to a blender with cold water, tequila, sugar, orange-flavored liqueur, and salt. blend until completely smooth, about 1 minute. strain mixture into a large measuring cup or small pitcher serve over ice and garnish with lime slices.

Strain mixture into a large measuring cup or small pitcher
Serve over ice and garnish with lime slices.

description: This cocojito (frozen mojito) recipe will teach you how to make a yummy frozen drink with slight coconut sweetness. Add more cream of coconut for a sweeter drink. Add more rum for a stronger one.
ingredients: 7 fluid ounces sweetened cream of coconut (such as Coco Lopez)
2 ½ (1.5 fluid ounce) jiggers white rum (such as Bacardi)
5 sprigs fresh mint leaves, more for garnish
directions: Place ice, cream of coconut, rum, 2½ limes, and 5 mint sprigs in a blender; blend until smooth. Pour into 2 glasses, garnish with mint sprigs and lime wedges.

description: We serve these when friends come over and they are always sure to liven up a party!
directions: Place ice in a blender and crush for 15 to 20 seconds. Add frozen strawberries, tequila, triple sec, and limeade. Blend until smooth.

description: This rhubarb margarita is a sweet-tart combo that is fabulous on a hot day!
directions: Bring rhubarb and water to a simmer in a saucepan, covered, over medium heat. Reduce heat to medium-low; continue simmering until rhubarb breaks down and releases its juices, about 15 minutes.

Strain rhubarb mixture through a fine-mesh sieve, pressing pulp to squeeze out as much juice as possible. Discard pulp. Stir sugar into hot juice. Refrigerate rhubarb syrup until very cold, at least 2 hours.
Place ice into a blender; pour in rhubarb syrup and tequila. Cover and blend until mixture is smooth or until your desired consistency. Pour into chilled margarita glasses.

description: The classic smooth, sweet, and tangy drink gets a makeover for summer!
ingredients: 1 (10 ounce) jar maraschino cherries, drained and stemmed
directions: Pour sweet and sour mix and lemon-lime beverage separately into empty ice cube trays and freeze until solid, about 6 hours.
Remove frozen cubes from trays.
Combine cubes, amaretto, and cherries in blender; blend on high until smooth.

recipe_title: Next Best Thing to Lulu's Piña Colada
description: Jimmy Buffett's sister, Lulu, owns a very popular restaurant in Gulf Shores, Alabama. Lulu's serves the absolute best piña colada in the world. This recipe is the next best thing to one of hers! If you can't find frozen mix, a ½ can of coconut cream and enough pineapple juice to create a simulated "can's worth" will work.
ingredients: 1 (10 ounce) can frozen piña colada mix
1 cup crushed pineapple in juice, undrained
directions: Combine rum, piña colada mix, crushed pineapple with juice, vanilla ice cream, and ice in a blender; blend until slushy.
Beat heavy cream in a small bowl with an electric mixer until soft peaks form; stir into piña colada mixture until evenly blended.
Divide piña colada among 4 glasses; top each with a cherry.
ingredient_text: bottle mango-flavored rum ounce can frozen piña colada mix crushed pineapple in juice undrained vanilla ice cream ice cubes ¼ heavy cream cherries with stems
directions_text: combine rum, piña colada mix, crushed pineapple with juice, vanilla ice cream, and ice in a blender; blend until slushy. beat heavy cream in a small bowl with an electric mixer until soft peaks form; stir into piña colada mixture until evenly blended. divide piña colada among 4 glasses; top each with a cherry.

Beat heavy cream in a small bowl with an electric mixer until soft peaks form; stir into piña colada mixture until evenly blended.
Divide piña colada among 4 glasses; top each with a cherry.
ingredients_canonical: ounce can frozen piña colada mix

description: This piña colada is made with three types of rum: dark, amber, and light. It's like a dream in a glass: 25% dessert, 75% tropical vacation, and 100% an adult beverage. You can hear the Pacific Ocean from Omaha, Nebraska if you have one of these in your hand. Enjoy!
ingredients: 2 fluid ounces sweet and sour mix
2 fluid ounces cream of coconut (such as Coco Lopez®)
directions: Pour light rum, amber rum, pineapple juice, sweet and sour mix, cream of coconut, and half-and-half cream into the pitcher of a blender with ice; blend until cocktail has a slushy texture. Pour slushy mixture into a glass.
Slowly and gently drizzle dark rum over the drink, so it just floats on top; serve.

description: This watermelon mojito recipe is perfect for summertime parties, BBQs, birthdays, and girls' nights. Is there anything more summery than a fresh, juicy watermelon? This drink is refreshing, delicious, and super easy to make on a hot day — not to mention, it looks beautiful in your glass! So, why not kick back, relax, and enjoy a tall glass of this mouthwatering cocktail as your stresses melt away? Garnish with lime and watermelon slices, plus fresh mint if desired.
ingredients: 2 cups ice cubes, or as needed
1 cup (8 fluid ounces) club soda, or as needed
directions: Place watermelon cubes into a blender; blend until smooth. Transfer to a mesh strainer set over a bowl to remove pulp. Measure 2 cups strained watermelon juice; set aside.
Place mint leaves in the bottom of a pitcher. Add ½ watermelon juice to the pitcher; mash mint leaves with a cocktail muddler or bottom of a wooden spoon.
Stir in remaining ½ watermelon juice, rum, lime juice, and simple syrup. Fill pitcher with ice; top with club soda to serve.

description: I love this homemade peach sangria recipe that my Grandfather taught me to make when I was a young wife. He learned to make when he was working in Mexico in his younger days. It's easy, but it takes time to cure between steps. We've had mango sangria, peach-mango sangria, or pear-pineapple sangria. You can garnish with blueberries, mint leaves, or fresh peach slices if you like.
ingredients: 4 large fresh peaches, peeled, pitted, and sliced
1 (750 milliliter) bottle sparkling white wine, chilled
directions: Place peaches, water, sugar, and cinnamon in a blender or the bowl of a food processor; blend until smooth. Transfer mixture to a pitcher; stir in dry white wine and triple sec. Cover the pitcher; chill in the refrigerator for at least 4 hours.
Strain peach mixture through a large cheesecloth-lined sieve; squeeze cloth to extrude all juice. Pour juice into a large pitcher; stir in sparkling wine.

description: Fresh strawberries paired with pineapple and lime make for a perfect drink on that first hot day of summer! Dark spiced rum adds a special flavor that stands up to the flavor burst you get from the fresh berries.
directions: Combine strawberries and sugar in blender; blend until smooth. Pour in pineapple juice, lime juice, and rum. Pulse until combined. Serve over ice.

ingredients: 2 tablespoons sweetened lime juice (such as Rose's®)
directions: Place the rum, lime juice, grenadine, confectioners' sugar, ice cubes, and strawberries into a blender. Cover, and puree until smooth. Pour into glasses and top each with a dollop of whipped cream, if desired.

description: I created this strawberry basil margarita recipe one night to use up some strawberries and basil. What a refreshing and delicious summer drink. Try it and see!
directions: Combine strawberries, tequila, basil, sugar, orange liqueur, and lemon juice in a blender; blend on low until smooth. Add ice; purée until crushed, 30 to 60 seconds.

description: Slushy, lemony, and deceptively strong, this refreshing lemon whiskey cocktail recipe is great for a summer evening.
directions: Combine Irish whiskey, lemon juice, and sugar in a blender; add ice cubes. Cover; blend until slushy, 15 to 30 seconds.
Pour slush into glasses.

directions_text: combine irish whiskey, lemon juice, and sugar in a blender; add ice cubes. cover; blend until slushy, 15 to 30 seconds. pour slush into glasses.

description: Peppermint margarita with a candy cane rim.
ingredients: ¼ cup red colored sugar, or as needed
¼ cup white sugar, or as needed
1 cup white tequila (such as Jose Cuervo Clasico®)
4 ½ fluid ounces peppermint schnapps, or to taste
8 cups ice cubes, or as needed
1 tablespoon grenadine syrup, or as desired
1 fluid ounce brandy-based orange liqueur (such as Grand Marnier®)
directions: Pour 1/4 to 1/2 inch of red sugar onto a small, shallow plate. Pour 1/4 to 1/2 inch of white sugar onto another small, shallow plate. Moisten the rims of margarita glasses with a wedge of lime, dip the moistened glasses into the red sugar, then the white, alternating sugars so that the edge of the glass looks like stripes on a candy cane; set aside.
Blend tequila, simple syrup, limeade, peppermint schnapps, triple sec, and lemon juice in a blender until smooth. Pour tequila mixture into a quart jar.

Fill blender with ice cubes. Pour 1/4 the tequila mixture over the ice and blend until smooth. Add grenadine for color while the mixture is blending. Pour mixture into prepared margarita glasses, add a splash of orange liqueur, and sprinkle crush candy canes over each margarita. Repeat with remaining tequila mixture and ice cubes.

description: A tropical, flavorful, alcoholic slush that packs a punch! Serve 2 scoops in a glass with lemon-lime soda. You'll want to keep this one away from the kids!
ingredients: 1 (46 fluid ounce) can unsweetened pineapple juice
directions: In a plastic container combine cream of coconut, lemonade concentrate, pineapple juice and vodka. Mix well and store overnight in the freezer.
To serve, place 2 scoops in a glass, then fill the glass with lemon-lime soda.

description: It's a Hawaiian vacation in a glass. You can't help but feel on vacation as you sip this tropical rum-based concoction. Be careful-this drink has a kick! You can substitute Triple Sec for the orange Curacao if you must.
ingredients: ¾ fluid ounce orange Curacao liqueur
2 fluid ounces sweet and sour mix
directions: Fill a large glass with ice; use a stir stick to mix in the light rum, amber rum, orange Curacao, pineapple juice, and sweet and sour mix.
Slowly and gently drizzle the dark rum on top of the cocktail so it floats on top of the drink.

description: Beer margaritas get a flavorful twist with strawberries! Change the flavors by changing the fruit. Mangoes or raspberries work nicely as well!
ingredients: 1 pound fresh strawberries, hulled and cut into chunks
1 (12 fluid ounce) can or bottle lemon-lime flavored carbonated beverage
1 (12 fluid ounce) can or bottle light beer
1 (12 fluid ounce) can frozen limeade concentrate, thawed
1 teaspoon kosher salt for rimming glasses
directions: Put strawberry chunks in a bowl. Pour tequila over strawberries. Marinate in refrigerator for 2 hours.
Blend strawberries and tequila in a blender until fully pureed; strain into a large pitcher to remove seeds.
Stir strained berry mixture, soda, beer, and limeade concentrate together in a pitcher. Add ice cubes to chill. Rim glasses with salt before pouring margaritas.

Blend strawberries and tequila in a blender until fully pureed; strain into a large pitcher to remove seeds.
Stir strained berry mixture, soda, beer, and limeade concentrate together in a pitcher. Add ice cubes to chill. Rim glasses with salt before pouring margaritas.

description: The blended Scotch version of the Manhattan. Named after the title character in a Sir Walter Scott novel, Rob Roy was a Robin Hood-like figure.
directions: Pour Scotch and vermouth into a glass with ice cubes. Shake in bitters, stir, and garnish with a maraschino cherry.

description: A fun authentic Hawaiian drink that tastes REALLY good. As you pour in the second mixture, the first will slowly creep up the sides of the glass like lava.
directions: Place strawberries, light rum, and dark rum into a blender. Puree until smooth and pour into a tall glass. Rinse out blender, then puree banana, pineapple juice, and cream of coconut until smooth. Slowly pour the banana mixture into the strawberry mixture and watch the lava rise!

description: I like blended and frozen drinks, so after trying the limited edition Kahlua® pumpkin spice holiday flavor I decided to see what I could come up with. It is delicious! Please use heavy whipping cream. It is available in small pint-sized containers. It is not much, but it adds a necessary creaminess to the drink texture. The pumpkin puree also adds to the pumpkin flavor. Feel free to garnish as desired with additional pumpkin spice, whipped cream, or both. Enjoy!
ingredients: ¼ cup pumpkin spice coffee-flavored liqueur (such as Kahlua®)
directions: Combine cream, milk, pumpkin puree, pumpkin liqueur, and pumpkin pie spice together in a blender. Add ice cubes. Blend until smooth, 30 to 45 seconds.

description: Setting out to duplicate the coconut margaritas we had in Costa Rica, my first attempt was awful! I surfed the internet for a good recipe with little luck. I then took what I'd learned from my first nasty try and the ideas I picked up online and after about 5 or 6 attempts I came up with this. Enjoy!
directions: Fill a blender with ice; add the coconut cream, tequila, and triple sec; blend at high speed until smooth. Scatter the coconut onto a plate. Wet the rims of four glasses. Dip the glass rims in the coconut. Serve the margaritas in the prepared glasses.

description: These peach margaritas are based on the easy margarita recipe on this site. That recipe uses limeade, tequila, Cointreau®, and ice. Garnish as desired.
ingredients: 2 tablespoons coarse sugar, or as needed
1 (16 ounce) package frozen unsweetened sliced peaches
2 fluid ounces orange-flavored liqueur (such as Cointreau®)
directions: Prepare margarita glasses by rubbing a lime wedge around the rims and dipping them in coarse sugar.

Place 1/2 of the ice into a blender, followed by 1/2 of the peaches, limeade, tequila, peach schnapps, and orange liqueur. Mix on high speed until well blended. Pour into the prepared margarita glasses. Blend remaining ingredients and pour into remaining glasses.

description: These drinks can be deceivingly strong. They are very sweet and fruity tasting. So don't drink and drive. Serve with great food and enjoy. I recommend using cherry or apricot brandy.
ingredients: 1 (6 ounce) can frozen orange juice concentrate
1 (12 fluid ounce) can frozen lemonade concentrate
67 fluid ounces lemon-lime flavored carbonated beverage
directions: Mix vodka, brandy, orange and lemon juice concentrate, cherries and grapefruit soda together in a large plastic mixing bowl. Cover the mixture with a tight fitting lid. Freeze the mixture for one or two days.
When you are ready to serve the drink, use an ice cream scoop and place one or two scoops of the mixture into glasses. Fill the rest of the glass with lemon/lime flavored soda pop. Stir and serve.

description: This spicy, savory, tangy, amazingly refreshing beer-based cocktail is considered one of the best hot-weather, adult beverages ever. I've heard it described as 'Bloody Mary meets Mimosa,' which makes me never want to go to brunch again, but it's also kind of accurate. You can use tomato juice or make your own using fresh cherry tomatoes.
ingredients: ¼ cup chopped red bell pepper
salt and ground black pepper to taste
directions: Place cherry tomatoes, celery, and red bell pepper into a blender. Pulse a few times to get mixture moving. Blend on high until mixture has liquefied, a few more seconds. Place strainer over a bowl and strain mixture to remove seeds and skins. Push mixture through strainer with a spatula to extract the liquid. Season with salt and pepper.
Sprinkle flake salt, black pepper, and chili pepper on a small plate. Rub rim of a glass with cut surface of a lime. Invert glass and dip rim into salt mixture.

Fill glass with ice and add Worcestershire sauce, hot sauce, and soy sauce. Pour in the vegetable juice and juice of 1/2 lime. Fill the glass 3/4 of the way with ice-cold beer. Stir. Top off with more beer. Garnish with lime slice.

description: Summer beer is a refreshing summertime cocktail that's great for special gatherings or cocktail parties. This recipe was given to me from Dottie, a long time friend and sorority sister. Garnish with lemon slices and sprigs of mint.

description: A Flaming Dr. Pepper is made by lighting a shot of rum and amaretto liqueur on fire before dropping it carefully into a glass of beer. It tastes just like Dr. Pepper itself, but be careful because the Bacardi 151 rum stands for 151 proof ⁠— that is why it is able to catch on fire!
directions: Fill a pint glass half full with beer.
Pour amaretto into a standard shot glass; pour rum on top so that it floats.
Light the shot on fire; carefully drop the lit shot into the half-full glass of beer and drink.

description: Depending on whom you talk to, making ice cream floats with beer is either a genius idea or a horrible mistake. The key is to choose a beer on the toasty, malty, sweeter side and stay away from beers that are too hoppy, dry, and bitter. Here I offer two recipe versions: coffee ice cream with stout and vanilla ice cream (the safest option) with a Belgian raspberry sour (or any fruity beer). I'd make a small test glass first to ensure it works for your palate.
directions: Version 1: Pour stout into a British pint glass and scoop coffee ice cream on top. Garnish with a dusting of cocoa powder.
Version 2: Pour raspberry beer into a pint glass; top with vanilla ice cream.
Add a straw to each float and serve with spoons alongside.

description: I'm lucky enough to live in an area with a large Hispanic population and what seems like a Mexican restaurant or taqueria on every corner. They each have their own michelada recipe, and this is my version of my favorite. Maggi®Jugo seasoning can be found in the international aisle of your grocery store or Hispanic markets and provides an umami flavor. You may substitute soy sauce if necessary. Add more hot sauce if you prefer!
ingredients: ¾ teaspoon soy-based liquid seasoning (such as Maggi® Jugo)
¾ teaspoon Mexican-style hot sauce (such as Valentina®)
3 fluid ounces tomato and clam juice cocktail (such as Clamato®)
1 (12 fluid ounce) bottle light Mexican beer (such as Modelo®)
directions: Pour Tajin® onto a small plate. Run 1 lime wedge around the edge of a pint glass, then invert glass onto the plate to create a salt rim.

Fill glass about halfway with ice. Squeeze the same lime wedge into the glass. Add Maggi® Jugo, hot sauce, and any remaining Tajin® from the plate and stir with a spoon. Pour in tomato and clam juice cocktail and top with beer. Garnish with remaining lime wedge and serve immediately.

description: This black and tan-colored beer cocktail recipe is a St. Patrick's Day staple at our house. Don't be fooled by the simple ingredients... The trick is getting them to layer just right. The key is to pour the stout slowly. Enjoy!
ingredients: 1 (12 fluid ounce) bottle lager beer (such as Harp®)
1 (12 fluid ounce) can or bottle Irish stout beer (such as Guinness®)
directions: Divide lager beer evenly between 2 tall beer glasses.
Working with one glass at a time, place a large tablespoon, dome-side up, 1 inch or so above lager beer, with the tip of the spoon pointed slightly downward.

Slowly pour 1/2 of the stout beer over the tablespoon so it gently pours down the side of the glass in a thick trickle. Allow to stand until 2 distinct layers of beer form, 3 to 5 seconds. Repeat with the remaining glass of beer.

description: Micheladas came from the words 'mi chela helada' which translates into 'my cold beer'. Micheladas originated in Mexico and now there are many different recipes. Dark beer, normally XX Cerveza is the norm in Mexico, but you can use any beer you like. This is a basic recipe with optional add-ons. With or without them, Micheladas are delicious. Garnish with a tamarind candy straw and additional lime wedges.
ingredients: 1 teaspoon chile-lime seasoning (such as Tajin®), or more to taste
½ cup tomato juice cocktail (such as Clamato®), chilled
1 teaspoon hot pepper sauce (such as Tapatio®)
1 (12 fluid ounce) can or bottle dark beer (such as Dos Equis XX Cerveza)
directions: Rub lime juice around the rim of a glass and dip in chile-lime seasoning. Fill with ice.
Combine tomato juice cocktail, chamoy, hot sauce, Worcestershire, soy sauce, and cayenne. Mix well and pour over ice. Fill the glass with beer.

description: Beer margaritas get a flavorful twist with strawberries! Change the flavors by changing the fruit. Mangoes or raspberries work nicely as well!
ingredients: 1 pound fresh strawberries, hulled and cut into chunks
1 (12 fluid ounce) can or bottle lemon-lime flavored carbonated beverage
1 (12 fluid ounce) can or bottle light beer
1 (12 fluid ounce) can frozen limeade concentrate, thawed

directions: Put strawberry chunks in a bowl. Pour tequila over strawberries. Marinate in refrigerator for 2 hours.
Blend strawberries and tequila in a blender until fully pureed; strain into a large pitcher to remove seeds.
Stir strained berry mixture, soda, beer, and limeade concentrate together in a pitcher. Add ice cubes to chill. Rim glasses with salt before pouring margaritas.

description: This spicy, savory, tangy, amazingly refreshing beer-based cocktail is considered one of the best hot-weather, adult beverages ever. I've heard it described as 'Bloody Mary meets Mimosa,' which makes me never want to go to brunch again, but it's also kind of accurate. You can use tomato juice or make your own using fresh cherry tomatoes.
ingredients: ¼ cup chopped red bell pepper
salt and ground black pepper to taste
directions: Place cherry tomatoes, celery, and red bell pepper into a blender. Pulse a few times to get mixture moving. Blend on high until mixture has liquefied, a few more seconds. Place strainer over a bowl and strain mixture to remove seeds and skins. Push mixture through strainer with a spatula to extract the liquid. Season with salt and pepper.
Sprinkle flake salt, black pepper, and chili pepper on a small plate. Rub rim of a glass with cut surface of a lime. Invert glass and dip rim into salt mixture.

Fill glass with ice and add Worcestershire sauce, hot sauce, and soy sauce. Pour in the vegetable juice and juice of 1/2 lime. Fill the glass 3/4 of the way with ice-cold beer. Stir. Top off with more beer. Garnish with lime slice.
ingredient_text: pints sweet cherry tomatoes ¼ chopped celery ¼ chopped red bell pepper salt ground black pepper taste
directions_text: place cherry tomatoes, celery, and red bell pepper into a blender. pulse a few times to get mixture moving. blend on high until mixture has liquefied, a few more seconds. place strainer over a bowl and strain mixture to remove seeds and skins. push mixture through strainer with a spatula to extract the liquid. season with salt and pepper. sprinkle flake salt, black pepper, and chili pepper on a small plate. rub rim of a glass with cut surface of a lime. invert glass and dip rim into salt mixture. fill glass with ice and add worcestershire sauce, hot sauce, and soy sauce. pour in the vegetable juice and juice of 1/2 lime. fill the glass 3/4 of the way with ice-cold beer. stir. top off with more beer. garnish with lime slice.
combined_text: chef john's "sunset" michelada cocktails beer cocktails 2 pints sweet cherry tomatoes ¼ cup chopped celery ¼ cup chopped red bell pepper salt and ground black pepper to taste place cherry tomatoes, celery, and red bell pepper into a blender. pulse a few times to get mixture moving. blend on high until mixture has liquefied, a few more seconds. place strainer over a bowl and strain mixture to remove seeds and skins. push mixture through strainer with a spatula to extract the liquid. season with salt and pepper. sprinkle flake salt, black pepper, and chili pepper on a small plate. rub rim of a glass with cut surface of a lime. invert glass and dip rim into salt mixture. fill glass with ice and add worcestershire sauce, hot sauce, and soy sauce. pour in the vegetable juice and juice of 1/2 lime. fill the glass 3/4 of the way with ice-cold beer. stir. top off with more beer. garnish with lime slice.

salt and ground black pepper to taste
directions_raw: Place cherry tomatoes, celery, and red bell pepper into a blender. Pulse a few times to get mixture moving. Blend on high until mixture has liquefied, a few more seconds. Place strainer over a bowl and strain mixture to remove seeds and skins. Push mixture through strainer with a spatula to extract the liquid. Season with salt and pepper.
Sprinkle flake salt, black pepper, and chili pepper on a small plate. Rub rim of a glass with cut surface of a lime. Invert glass and dip rim into salt mixture.

Fill glass with ice and add Worcestershire sauce, hot sauce, and soy sauce. Pour in the vegetable juice and juice of 1/2 lime. Fill the glass 3/4 of the way with ice-cold beer. Stir. Top off with more beer. Garnish with lime slice.

description: This yummy margaritas recipe is simply frozen lime margaritas made with beer.
ingredients: 1 (12 fluid ounce) can frozen limeade concentrate
directions: Combine limeade, beer, and tequila in a blender. Fill with ice. Blend until thick and smooth.
Pour salt onto a small plate, moisten rims of cocktail glasses with a damp towel; press rims into salt.
Pour margarita into prepared glasses; garnish with a lime slice and serve.

description: This rosemary simple syrup is a sweet herbal syrup with the essence of a Mediterranean summer. Stir into lemonade, flavor a cocktail, glaze a cake, or churn it into a sorbet. So refreshing!
directions: Combine water, sugar, and rosemary leaves in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil, stirring until sugar dissolves. Simmer for 1 minute. Remove from heat and let syrup steep for 30 minutes.
Pour syrup into a sterilized glass jar through a mesh strainer to remove rosemary leaves; let cool.

description: This raspberry syrup recipe is great for flavoring iced tea but can also be used in cocktails, soda, and other drinks.
directions: Bring water and sugar to a boil in a small saucepan, stirring occasionally, until sugar is dissolved, 5 to 10 minutes. Remove from heat, stir in raspberries, and mash with a potato masher or fork. Let steep for at least 1 hour.
Drain mixture through a fine mesh strainer, pressing raspberry pulp with a spatula to extract as much liquid as possible. Discard solids and store syrup in a sealed container in the refrigerator.

description: Use this basil simple syrup to flavor summer's most refreshing cocktails, mocktails, lemonades, and frozen treats. Fresh basil leaves are steeped in sugar and water to create a sweet herbal syrup.
directions: Combine water, sugar, and basil leaves in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil, stirring until sugar dissolves. Reduce heat and simmer for 1 minute. Remove from heat and let syrup steep for 30 minutes.
Drain syrup through a mesh strainer into a sterilized glass jar; discard basil leaves. Let cool, then cover and store in the refrigerator for up to 1 month.

description: This homemade cinnamon dolce syrup is rich and delicious. Use this to make a latte or add to apple juice or cider for the perfect fall drink!
ingredients: ¾ cup packed light brown sugar
directions: Place a saucepan over medium-high heat; add water, brown sugar, and white sugar. Whisk until sugar dissolves, 3 to 5 minutes. Whisk cinnamon into sugar mixture until incorporated. Continue whisking mixture until just before boiling and syrup-consistency, 3 to 5 more minutes. Remove saucepan from heat and stir in vanilla extract.
Cool syrup completely, at least 1 hour. Pour syrup into a glass container and seal; store in refrigerator for up to 10 days.

directions_text: place a saucepan over medium-high heat; add water, brown sugar, and white sugar. whisk until sugar dissolves, 3 to 5 minutes. whisk cinnamon into sugar mixture until incorporated. continue whisking mixture until just before boiling and syrup-consistency, 3 to 5 more minutes. remove saucepan from heat and stir in vanilla extract. cool syrup completely, at least 1 hour. pour syrup into a glass container and seal; store in refrigerator for up to 10 days.

recipe_title: Billy's Favorite Gingerbread Spiced Coffee Syrup
description: This is a delightful spicy syrup that you can use to spice up coffee, tea, apple cider, you name it, it's all good! Add a tablespoon to your favorite beverage, or use it in whipped cream, or brush it on cakes. Drizzle it on yogurt, oatmeal, or pancakes - the possibilities are endless!
ingredients: 1 (1 inch) piece fresh ginger root, sliced
1 cinnamon stick, broken into large pieces
directions: In a saucepan over medium-high heat, combine water, sugar, and honey. Stir in ginger, cinnamon stick, cloves, allspice, peppercorns, and nutmeg; bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 25 to 30 minutes. Let cool for 20 minutes, then strain through a fine mesh sieve, or double layer cheesecloth.

description: This hibiscus syrup is sweet, tart, and floral. Perfect for keeping on hand for adding extra zing and beautiful ruby red color to margaritas, rum punches, and daiquiris. Or just pour over ice and add club soda for a delicious fresh soda.
ingredients: 1 (1/2 inch) piece fresh ginger root, thinly sliced
directions: Bring water, white sugar, hibiscus, brown sugar, ginger, and lemon zest to a boil. Reduce heat and let simmer until sugars dissolve and flowers soften, about 10 minutes. Remove from heat and steep syrup until flavors combine, about 5 minutes.
Strain syrup into a container through a fine-mesh strainer, pressing solids with a spoon to extract as much liquid as possible. Discard solids.
Store syrup in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

description: I love coffee. Not just hot coffee, but cold and frozen coffee drinks, coffee candy, coffee baked goods, my coffee rub, I can go on and on. It is the deep, earthy smell and the taste of good coffee that makes me smile. This coffee syrup recipe is so simple to make and very versatile. Use good-quality ground coffee and not instant coffee for best results. Once made, store the syrup in the fridge for up to two weeks and use to flavor milk, baked goods, in cocktails, or over pancakes.
directions: Combine sugar, water, and ground coffee in a saucepan over medium heat; cook, stirring often, until sugar dissolves and mixture comes to a rolling boil, about 10 minutes. Reduce heat to low; cook, stirring occasionally, for 10 minutes.
Remove from heat; cool, about 30 minutes.
Strain syrup through a fine-mesh sieve into a lidded container; cover. Store in the refrigerator.

directions_text: combine sugar, water, and ground coffee in a saucepan over medium heat; cook, stirring often, until sugar dissolves and mixture comes to a rolling boil, about 10 minutes. reduce heat to low; cook, stirring occasionally, for 10 minutes. remove from heat; cool, about 30 minutes. strain syrup through a fine-mesh sieve into a lidded container; cover. store in the refrigerator.

description: Habanero simple syrup!
directions: Bring water to a boil in a saucepan. Slowly stir sugar into boiling water until sugar is dissolved, 2 to 3 minutes. Add habanero peppers to sugar-water mixture, reduce heat to medium-low, and simmer until syrup is thickened and pepper has slightly colored the syrup, about 15 minutes.
Remove saucepan from heat and let syrup cool, about 1 hour. Remove peppers from syrup and store syrup in a glass jar.

description: Simple Syrup is brushed on cakes for added moisture and flavor. This one can be flavored with brandy or rum.
directions: In a saucepan, combine water, sugar and lemon zest. Bring mixture to a boil and continue boiling for 5 minutes. Strain into a sealable container, removing the lemon zest. Allow to cool, then cover and store in refrigerator.
When ready to use, mix in brandy. Brush onto cake layers before frosting.

Pour syrup through a mesh strainer into a sterilized glass jar to remove lemon verbena leaves; let cool.

recipe_title: Simple Syrup with Truvia Natural Sweetener
description: This simple syrup sweetened with Truvia natural sweetener recipe provides a savings of 100 calories or 26 grams of sugar per serving compared to the sugar-sweetened version.
ingredients: 3 ½ tablespoons spoonable Truvia natural sweetener
directions: Add Truvia natural sweetener to ½ cup warm water in a sterilized lidded jar. Cover and shake or blend until completely dissolved.
Store in the refrigerator. For best results, use within 1 day of making.

description: This pumpkin spice syrup lets you make pumpkin spice lattes at home that taste just like you get at Starbucks! This recipe is so good, that I wanted to share it.
directions: Mix water and sugar together in a saucepan; bring to a boil. Reduce the heat to medium-low and cook, stirring regularly, until sugar is completely dissolved completely, about 5 minutes.

Stir 6 tablespoons pumpkin pie filling, cinnamon sticks, nutmeg, ginger, cloves, vanilla, and pumpkin pie spice into the sugar syrup; bring to a simmer. Cook until mixture is well spiced, 8 to 10 minutes.
Strain mixture through a sheet of cheesecloth into a bowl. Return liquid to the saucepan over low heat.
Stir condensed milk and remaining 2 tablespoons pumpkin pie filling into the spiced syrup. Cook, stirring regularly, until pumpkin dissolves into the liquid, about 5 minutes.
ingredient_text: ½ water ½ white sugar pumpkin pie filling divided cinnamon sticks freshly grated nutmeg minced fresh ginger whole cloves vanilla extract ½ pumpkin pie spice ¼ sweetened condensed milk
directions_text: mix water and sugar together in a saucepan; bring to a boil. reduce the heat to medium-low and cook, stirring regularly, until sugar is completely dissolved completely, about 5 minutes. stir 6 tablespoons pumpkin pie filling, cinnamon sticks, nutmeg, ginger, cloves, vanilla, and pumpkin pie spice into the sugar syrup; bring to a simmer. cook until mixture is well spiced, 8 to 10 minutes. strain mixture through a sheet of cheesecloth into a bowl. return liquid to the saucepan over low heat. stir condensed milk and remaining 2 tablespoons pumpkin pie filling into the spiced syrup. cook, stirring regularly, until pumpkin dissolves into the liquid, about 5 minutes.
