Stir 6 tablespoons pumpkin pie filling, cinnamon sticks, nutmeg, ginger, cloves, vanilla, and pumpkin pie spice into the sugar syrup; bring to a simmer. Cook until mixture is well spiced, 8 to 10 minutes.
Strain mixture through a sheet of cheesecloth into a bowl. Return liquid to the saucepan over low heat.
Stir condensed milk and remaining 2 tablespoons pumpkin pie filling into the spiced syrup. Cook, stirring regularly, until pumpkin dissolves into the liquid, about 5 minutes.

description: Delicious and easy to make honey syrup for pancakes! Do you want a morning snack like pancakes, but the syrup has a lot of sugar? Well, this has only honey!
directions: Combine milk and vanilla extract in a bowl.
Heat honey and butter together in a microwave-safe bowl in the microwave for 30 seconds; stir into milk mixture.
Allow to cool completely before use. Chill in the refrigerator if too hot.
ingredient_text: milk vanilla extract honey butter
directions_text: combine milk and vanilla extract in a bowl. heat honey and butter together in a microwave-safe bowl in the microwave for 30 seconds; stir into milk mixture. allow to cool completely before use. chill in the refrigerator if too hot.
combined_text: vanilla honey simple syrup cocktails drink flavoring & simple syrups 2 teaspoons milk 2 teaspoons vanilla extract 1 tablespoon honey 2 teaspoons butter combine milk and vanilla extract in a bowl. heat honey and butter together in a microwave-safe bowl in the microwave for 30 seconds; stir into milk mixture. allow to cool completely before use. chill in the refrigerator if too hot.
directions_raw: Combine milk and vanilla extract in a bowl.
Heat honey and butter together in a microwave-safe bowl in the microwave for 30 seconds; stir into milk mixture.
Allow to cool completely before use. Chill in the refrigerator if too hot.

description: It's extremely easy to make homemade grenadine. It's a two-ingredient operation. Your cocktails will thank you.
directions: Pour sugar and pomegranate juice into a sauce pan and warm over low heat, whisking continuously until the sugar dissolves. Cool, pour into a bottle or jar, and store sealed in the refrigerator.
ingredient_text: white sugar ½ pomegranate juice
directions_text: pour sugar and pomegranate juice into a sauce pan and warm over low heat, whisking continuously until the sugar dissolves. cool, pour into a bottle or jar, and store sealed in the refrigerator.
combined_text: homemade grenadine cocktails drink flavoring & simple syrups 1 cup white sugar ½ cup pomegranate juice pour sugar and pomegranate juice into a sauce pan and warm over low heat, whisking continuously until the sugar dissolves. cool, pour into a bottle or jar, and store sealed in the refrigerator.
directions_raw: Pour sugar and pomegranate juice into a sauce pan and warm over low heat, whisking continuously until the sugar dissolves. Cool, pour into a bottle or jar, and store sealed in the refrigerator.

description: Add some spice to your syrup using this easy variation.
ingredients: ¼ cup coarsely chopped unpeeled fresh ginger
directions: Mix together water, sugar, ginger, lemon juice, and peppercorns in a saucepan. Bring to a boil; reduce heat and simmer, stirring frequently, until sugar dissolves. Let cool, 15 to 30 minutes.
Strain syrup through a fine-mesh sieve; discard solids. Chill, covered, for up to 2 weeks.
ingredient_text: ½ water white sugar ¼ coarsely chopped unpeeled fresh ginger freshly squeezed lemon juice ½ whole black peppercorns
directions_text: mix together water, sugar, ginger, lemon juice, and peppercorns in a saucepan. bring to a boil; reduce heat and simmer, stirring frequently, until sugar dissolves. let cool, 15 to 30 minutes. strain syrup through a fine-mesh sieve; discard solids. chill, covered, for up to 2 weeks.
combined_text: ginger syrup cocktails drink flavoring & simple syrups 1 ½ cups water 1 cup white sugar ¼ cup coarsely chopped unpeeled fresh ginger 2 tablespoons freshly squeezed lemon juice ½ teaspoon whole black peppercorns mix together water, sugar, ginger, lemon juice, and peppercorns in a saucepan. bring to a boil; reduce heat and simmer, stirring frequently, until sugar dissolves. let cool, 15 to 30 minutes. strain syrup through a fine-mesh sieve; discard solids. chill, covered, for up to 2 weeks.
ingredients_raw: ¼ cup coarsely chopped unpeeled fresh ginger
directions_raw: Mix together water, sugar, ginger, lemon juice, and peppercorns in a saucepan. Bring to a boil; reduce heat and simmer, stirring frequently, until sugar dissolves. Let cool, 15 to 30 minutes.
Strain syrup through a fine-mesh sieve; discard solids. Chill, covered, for up to 2 weeks.
ingredients_canonical: ¼ coarsely chopped unpeeled fresh ginger

description: Drizzle this sweet and floral syrup in your lemonade, sparkling water, or iced tea. Mix with vodka and soda or gin and tonic for a fancy craft cocktail. This also makes a delicious latte!
directions: Combine water, blueberries, sugar, and honey in a saucepan over medium-high heat. Bring to a boil, then reduce temperature to a light simmer. Add in lavender, stir to combine, and simmer for 10 minutes.
Let mixture cool for 10 minutes, then strain into a jar using a fine mesh strainer. Use the back of a spoon to mash the berries through the strainer to release juices. Refrigerate for up to 1 month.
ingredient_text: water frozen blueberries ¾ sugar ¼ honey ½ dried lavender buds
directions_text: combine water, blueberries, sugar, and honey in a saucepan over medium-high heat. bring to a boil, then reduce temperature to a light simmer. add in lavender, stir to combine, and simmer for 10 minutes. let mixture cool for 10 minutes, then strain into a jar using a fine mesh strainer. use the back of a spoon to mash the berries through the strainer to release juices. refrigerate for up to 1 month.
combined_text: blueberry-lavender simple syrup cocktails drink flavoring & simple syrups 1 cup water 1 cup frozen blueberries ¾ cup sugar ¼ cup honey 1 ½ tablespoons dried lavender buds combine water, blueberries, sugar, and honey in a saucepan over medium-high heat. bring to a boil, then reduce temperature to a light simmer. add in lavender, stir to combine, and simmer for 10 minutes. let mixture cool for 10 minutes, then strain into a jar using a fine mesh strainer. use the back of a spoon to mash the berries through the strainer to release juices. refrigerate for up to 1 month.
directions_raw: Combine water, blueberries, sugar, and honey in a saucepan over medium-high heat. Bring to a boil, then reduce temperature to a light simmer. Add in lavender, stir to combine, and simmer for 10 minutes.
Let mixture cool for 10 minutes, then strain into a jar using a fine mesh strainer. Use the back of a spoon to mash the berries through the strainer to release juices. Refrigerate for up to 1 month.

description: This is a super easy-to-make syrup that combines the best flavors of pumpkin pie into a syrup wonderful for pancakes, especially pumpkin pancakes!
directions: Bring corn syrup, water, brown sugar, and butter to a rolling boil. Whisk pumpkin, ginger, nutmeg, allspice, and cinnamon into corn syrup mixture; cook, stirring constantly, until flavors blend, 5 to 10 minutes. Serve hot.
ingredient_text: light corn syrup water ½ brown sugar ½ butter ⅓ pumpkin puree ground ginger ground nutmeg ground allspice ground cinnamon
directions_text: bring corn syrup, water, brown sugar, and butter to a rolling boil. whisk pumpkin, ginger, nutmeg, allspice, and cinnamon into corn syrup mixture; cook, stirring constantly, until flavors blend, 5 to 10 minutes. serve hot.
combined_text: pumpkin spice syrup cocktails drink flavoring & simple syrups 1 cup light corn syrup 1 cup water ½ cup brown sugar ½ cup butter ⅓ cup pumpkin puree 1 teaspoon ground ginger 1 teaspoon ground nutmeg 1 teaspoon ground allspice 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon bring corn syrup, water, brown sugar, and butter to a rolling boil. whisk pumpkin, ginger, nutmeg, allspice, and cinnamon into corn syrup mixture; cook, stirring constantly, until flavors blend, 5 to 10 minutes. serve hot.
directions_raw: Bring corn syrup, water, brown sugar, and butter to a rolling boil. Whisk pumpkin, ginger, nutmeg, allspice, and cinnamon into corn syrup mixture; cook, stirring constantly, until flavors blend, 5 to 10 minutes. Serve hot.

description: This syrup is sweet and tart, and is a festive addition to holiday cocktails!
directions: Dissolve sugar in water in a saucepan over medium heat. Add cranberries and simmer on low until cranberries have softened but before they begin to pop, about 15 minutes. Cool, strain, and refrigerate.
ingredient_text: white sugar water ½ fresh cranberries
directions_text: dissolve sugar in water in a saucepan over medium heat. add cranberries and simmer on low until cranberries have softened but before they begin to pop, about 15 minutes. cool, strain, and refrigerate.
combined_text: cranberry simple syrup cocktails drink flavoring & simple syrups 1 cup white sugar 1 cup water 2 ½ cups fresh cranberries dissolve sugar in water in a saucepan over medium heat. add cranberries and simmer on low until cranberries have softened but before they begin to pop, about 15 minutes. cool, strain, and refrigerate.
directions_raw: Dissolve sugar in water in a saucepan over medium heat. Add cranberries and simmer on low until cranberries have softened but before they begin to pop, about 15 minutes. Cool, strain, and refrigerate.

description: This rich, tawny syrup will elevate your cocktails from 'pretty in pink' to 'pretty spectacular'. Shop around for pomegranate juice! There are a few options for 100% unsweetened juices, and I haven't had bad results at any price point.
directions: Combine 3 cups pomegranate juice and sugar together in a saucepan; heat to 244 degrees F (118 degrees C) or until a small amount of mixture dropped into cold water forms a firm ball. Remove saucepan from heat and cover with a lid; let cool until pan is cool enough to touch. Stir remaining 1 cup pomegranate juice into mixture. Refrigerate.
ingredient_text: pomegranate juice divided white sugar
directions_text: combine 3 cups pomegranate juice and sugar together in a saucepan; heat to 244 degrees f (118 degrees c) or until a small amount of mixture dropped into cold water forms a firm ball. remove saucepan from heat and cover with a lid; let cool until pan is cool enough to touch. stir remaining 1 cup pomegranate juice into mixture. refrigerate.
combined_text: pomegranate grenadine cocktails drink flavoring & simple syrups 4 cups pomegranate juice, divided 1 cup white sugar combine 3 cups pomegranate juice and sugar together in a saucepan; heat to 244 degrees f (118 degrees c) or until a small amount of mixture dropped into cold water forms a firm ball. remove saucepan from heat and cover with a lid; let cool until pan is cool enough to touch. stir remaining 1 cup pomegranate juice into mixture. refrigerate.
directions_raw: Combine 3 cups pomegranate juice and sugar together in a saucepan; heat to 244 degrees F (118 degrees C) or until a small amount of mixture dropped into cold water forms a firm ball. Remove saucepan from heat and cover with a lid; let cool until pan is cool enough to touch. Stir remaining 1 cup pomegranate juice into mixture. Refrigerate.

description: Foolproof chocolate caramel syrup makes a wonderful exciting latte! Better than you know who!
directions: In a saucepan, stir together the cream, corn syrup, white sugar, brown sugar and salt. Bring to a rolling boil over medium-high heat and continue to boil until it becomes thick and deep brown, 8 to 10 minutes. Remove from the heat and stir in the chocolate and butter until smooth.
ingredient_text: heavy cream light corn syrup ½ white sugar ½ packed brown sugar ⅛ salt ounces milk chocolate chopped ¼ butter
directions_text: in a saucepan, stir together the cream, corn syrup, white sugar, brown sugar and salt. bring to a rolling boil over medium-high heat and continue to boil until it becomes thick and deep brown, 8 to 10 minutes. remove from the heat and stir in the chocolate and butter until smooth.
combined_text: chocolate caramel latte syrup cocktails drink flavoring & simple syrups 1 cup heavy cream 1 cup light corn syrup ½ cup white sugar ½ cup packed brown sugar ⅛ teaspoon salt 8 ounces milk chocolate, chopped ¼ cup butter in a saucepan, stir together the cream, corn syrup, white sugar, brown sugar and salt. bring to a rolling boil over medium-high heat and continue to boil until it becomes thick and deep brown, 8 to 10 minutes. remove from the heat and stir in the chocolate and butter until smooth.
directions_raw: In a saucepan, stir together the cream, corn syrup, white sugar, brown sugar and salt. Bring to a rolling boil over medium-high heat and continue to boil until it becomes thick and deep brown, 8 to 10 minutes. Remove from the heat and stir in the chocolate and butter until smooth.
